Prayer and Inheritance for All Muslims
Statement on funeral prayer and inheritance for all Muslims.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that the Ahl al-Sunnah pray over every deceased Muslim, regardless of their piety, and uphold inheritance laws among all Muslims.

Core Idea
Every Muslim, righteous or sinful, deserves prayer and inheritance.

Imam al-Ash'ari continues outlining the beliefs of Ahl al-Sunnah. Here he addresses two practical matters: funeral prayer and inheritance. These rulings reflect the unity of the Muslim community and the principle that faith itself, not personal merit, is the basis for these rights. Let's examine this concise but weighty statement.

Guided Path
1
Read the Arabic passage and translation
Read the Arabic sentence slowly, then the translation. Notice the concise style of the matn.
The passage uses 'نَّدين' meaning 'we hold as a religious duty'.
2
Identify the two rulings
The passage contains two distinct beliefs: (1) performing funeral prayer for all Muslims, (2) affirming inheritance among them. Identify the Arabic words for each.
The word 'توارث' means mutual inheritance.
3
Reflect on the inclusive principle
Contrast this with any notion that sinful Muslims are excluded from funeral prayer or inheritance. Note how this upholds the unity of the Ummah.
This principle is rooted in the idea that faith itself, not individual sin, defines membership.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
٦٣ - وندين بالصلاة على من مات من أهل القبلة، برهم وفَاجِرهم، وتوارثهم
63 - And we hold the duty of praying over whoever dies from the People of the Qibla, their righteous and their sinful, and (we affirm) inheritance among them.
Source pages p. 55

Commentary
This passage sets forth two related beliefs: first, we perform the funeral prayer over every person who dies while belonging to the People of the Qibla (i.e., Muslims), regardless of whether they were righteous or sinful. Second, we affirm that inheritance laws apply among them.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?

Waki's Ruling on Those Who Say the Quran is Created
Waki' declares the one who says the Quran is created an apostate.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the severe position of early Sunni scholar Waki' ibn al-Jarrah regarding the doctrine of the created Quran, including the declaration of apostasy and the procedure of istitaba.

Core Idea
Saying the Quran is created was considered apostasy by early Sunni scholars.

In this passage, we encounter a powerful statement from the early scholar Waki' ibn al-Jarrah. He leaves no room for ambiguity: the one who claims the Quran is created has left the fold of Islam. This lesson unpacks the gravity of that judgment and its implications for early Islamic orthodoxy.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage and its translation
Begin by reading the Arabic passage and the literal English translation. Notice the concise style of the report and the key terms used.
The passage is a short athar (report) from a companion of Waki'.
2
Identify the key terms
Look for the terms 'مخلوق' (created), 'مرتد' (apostate), and 'يستتاب' (he is asked to repent). Understand their meanings and significance.
These terms are central to the ruling.
3
Understand the legal procedure
Waki' prescribes a procedure: first, the person is asked to repent; if they do, they are left; if not, they are executed. Recognize that this is the standard Islamic punishment for apostasy.
Istitaba (asking to repent) is a requirement before execution.
4
Reflect on the doctrinal context
Consider why this issue was so grave. The createdness of the Quran was a major controversy between the Mu'tazila and Ahl al-Sunnah. This statement shows the firm stance of early Sunni scholars.
The Quran's uncreatedness is a foundational Sunni belief.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
وذكر الحسين بن عبد الأول، قال: سمعت وكيعاً يقول : من قال: القرآن مخلوق فهو مرتد يستتاب فإن تاب وإلا قتل .
And al-Husayn ibn 'Abd al-Awwal mentioned, he said: I heard Waki' saying: Whoever says the Quran is created is an apostate; he is asked to repent; if he repents, [good], otherwise he is killed.
Source pages p. 87

Commentary
This passage is a report (athar) from Waki' (ibn al-Jarrah), an early Islamic scholar, stating the ruling regarding those who assert that the Quran is created. He declares such a person to be a murtadd (apostate), meaning they have left the fold of Islam. The prescribed procedure is istitaba (asking for repentance). If the person repents, they are left alone; if not, the punishment is execution. This reflects the strict position of the early Salaf on the issue of the createdness of the Quran.

Key Terms
مرتد
خلق القرآن
استتابة

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some think that the early scholars only considered this a minor innovation, but this shows it was viewed as apostasy.
Correction: Waki' explicitly states that saying the Quran is created makes one an apostate, subject to the severe punishment of execution if unrepentant.

Checkpoint
According to Waki', what is the ruling on someone who says the Quran is created?
A) They are a sinner but still Muslim
B) They are an apostate and must be executed if they do not repent
C) They are simply mistaken and should be debated
D) They are a disbeliever but not punished

Key Takeaway
Waki' considered the belief that the Quran is created as apostasy, requiring repentance or execution.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
Waki's ruling treats a wrong belief about the Quran as apostasy. Are there any beliefs you hold about Allah or His attributes that you have not verified from the Quran and Sunnah? What will you do today to check them?
?
If you were to meet someone who says the Quran is created, would you treat the matter as serious as apostasy? Why or why not?
?
The early scholars were willing to execute those who refused to repent from this belief. Are you as firm in defending the truth of the Quran as they were? What is one step you can take this week to strengthen your conviction in the Quran as the uncreated speech of Allah?

Faith and Disbelief
beginner
3 min
The Created Quran as Disbelief
Al-Ash'ari quotes Sufyan al-Thawri declaring the createdness of the Quran as disbelief.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the early Sunni position that declaring the Quran to be created constitutes disbelief, as expressed by Sufyan al-Thawri.

Core Idea
Calling the Quran created is not just error—it is disbelief.

This short but powerful statement from the early scholar Sufyan al-Thawri draws a clear line in the sand. Al-Ash'ari includes it to show that the doctrine of the created Quran was not a minor disagreement but a matter of fundamental creed. Let's examine what this report means and why it matters.

Guided Path
1
Read the report
Pay attention to the chain of narration and the direct statement. Notice that al-Thawri says 'whoever claims... has disbelieved.'
The chain indicates reliable transmission.
2
Identify the ruling
The word 'kafara' (disbelieved) is a strong term. Understand that this is not just a mistake but an act that removes one from the fold of faith.
3
Consider the context
Why would al-Thawri make such a strong statement? Reflect on the gravity of attributing creation to the eternal speech of Allah.
This relates to the doctrine of divine attributes.
4
Reflect on implications
What does this teach us about the seriousness of theological errors? It shows that early scholars saw creedal deviations as matters of disbelief.
This is a key principle in Sunni orthodoxy.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
١٢٨ وروى الليث بن يحيى، قال: حدثني إبراهيم بن أبي الأشعث، قال: سمعت مؤمل بن إسماعيل يحدث عن الثوري، قال: من زعم أن القرآن مخلوق فقد کفر
128 – And al-Layth bin Yahya narrated: Ibrahim bin Abi al-Ash'ath narrated to me: I heard Mu'ammal bin Isma'il narrating from al-Thawri who said: Whoever claims that the Quran is created has indeed disbelieved.
Source pages p. 90

Commentary
This passage is a statement from the early authority Sufyan al-Thawri. Al-Ash'ari uses it to show that the doctrine of the created Quran is not just an error but tantamount to disbelief (kufr). Al-Thawri's verdict places the issue within the realm of fundamental creed. The strong language indicates that affirming the Quran as created contradicts core Islamic belief in the divine attributes. This statement serves as a precedent for anathematizing those who hold this view, underlining the seriousness of the matter in early Sunni orthodoxy.

Key Terms
القرآن مخلوق (Quran created)
كفر (disbelief)
زعم (claimed)

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some think the condemnation of the created Quran doctrine was a later development.
Correction: Al-Ash'ari shows it was the position of early scholars like Sufyan al-Thawri.

Checkpoint
According to Sufyan al-Thawri, what is the ruling on someone who says the Quran is created?
A) He is a sinner but still a believer
B) He has disbelieved (kufr)
C) He is misguided but not a disbeliever
D) He should be punished but not excommunicated

Key Takeaway
For Sunni orthodoxy, the belief that the Quran is created is not a minor error but a matter of disbelief.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
Do you personally affirm that the Quran is the uncreated speech of Allah? If not, what is holding you back from accepting this foundational belief of Ahl al-Sunnah?
?
Imagine standing before Allah and being asked about your stance on the Quran's nature. Would you confidently say it is His eternal word, or do you have any doubt that needs to be resolved?
?
This passage uses strong language—'disbelieved.' Does the gravity of this issue cause you to examine your own heart and ensure you hold the correct belief about the Quran? What step will you take today to strengthen your conviction?

Eschatological Beliefs of Ahl al-Sunnah
Al-Ash'ari details beliefs on the afterlife: intercession, grave punishment, hawd, mizan, sirat, resurrection, and judgment.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the core eschatological beliefs affirmed by Imam al-Ash'ari, including intercession, punishment of the grave, the Basin, the Scale, the Path, resurrection, and the final judgment.

Core Idea
The afterlife is real and detailed: intercession, grave, scales, and path all await.

Imam al-Ash'ari presents a concise summary of Sunni beliefs about the afterlife. These are not abstract ideas but concrete realities that every believer must affirm based on revelation. This passage covers the key events from death to final judgment, emphasizing hope and fear for sinners and the role of prophetic intercession.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage carefully
Read the Arabic passage once without trying to analyse it. Then read the translation. Notice the list of beliefs and the careful phrasing about hope and fear.
The passage begins by clarifying that we do not make definitive judgments about individuals except those explicitly promised Paradise.
2
Identify the list of eschatological events
After discussing intercession, the passage lists: punishment of the grave, the Basin, the Scale, the Path, resurrection, standing, and accounting. Count them and reflect on their order.
3
Understand the nuance about intercession
Al-Ash'ari specifies that intercession brings out people who have been 'charred' – highlighting that even severe sinners may be saved through the Prophet's intercession.
This balances hope and fear: we do not presume salvation for all, but we do not despair of Allah's mercy.
4
Reflect on the purpose of affirming these beliefs
Consider why al-Ash'ari includes these details. They ground our hope and fear in concrete events ordained by Allah and attested by revelation.
This passage is part of al-Ibana, a work defending Sunni orthodoxy against rationalist denials.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
وندين بأن لا ننزل أحداً من أهل التوحيد والمتمسكين بالإيمان جنة ولا ناراً إلا من شهد له رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم بالجنة. ونرجو الجنة للمذنبين ونخاف عليهم أن يكونوا بالنار معذبين . ونقول : إن الله عز وجل يخرج قوماً من النار بعد أن امتحشوا بشفاعة الرسول صلى الله عليه وسلم تصديقاً لما جاءت به الروايات عن الرسول صلى الله عليه وسلم . ونؤمن بعذاب القبر ، وبالحوض . وأن الميزان حق ، والصراط حق ، والبعث بعد الموت حق ، وأن الله عز وجل يوقف العباد في الموقف ويحاسب المؤمنين .
And we hold as religion that we do not assign anyone of the people of tawhid (monotheism) and those who adhere to faith to Paradise or Hellfire except for one whom the Messenger of Allah, peace be upon him, testified to be in Paradise. And we hope for Paradise for the sinners and fear for them that they will be punished in the Fire. And we say: Allah, Mighty and Majestic, will bring out a people from Hellfire after they have been charred, by the intercession of the Messenger, peace be upon him, in confirmation of what the narrations from the Messenger came with. And we believe in the punishment of the grave, and in the Basin (al-hawd). And that the Scale (al-mizan) is true, and the Path (al-sirat) is true, and the resurrection after death is true, and that Allah, Mighty and Majestic, will stop the servants at the standing-place and will call the believers to account.
Source pages p. 24

Commentary
This passage is a comprehensive statement of eschatological beliefs. It begins by stating that we do not make definitive judgments about who goes to Paradise or Hell except for those explicitly promised Paradise by the Prophet (such as the ten companions). For other believers, we hope for mercy but also fear punishment. Intercession (shafa'a) is affirmed: some people will be brought out of Hellfire after being reduced to cinders, through the Prophet's intercession. This is based on authentic hadith. Then the passage lists several core beliefs: the punishment of the grave (عذاب القبر), the Basin (الحوض) of the Prophet on the Day of Judgment, the Scale (الميزان) weighing deeds, the Path (الصراط) over Hellfire, resurrection (البعث) after death, and the standing (الموقف) where Allah will hold the believers to account. All these are accepted as true based on revelation.

Key Terms
شفاعة
عذاب القبر
حوض
ميزان
صراط
بعث
موقف
محاسبة

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some deny intercession or specific afterlife events like the punishment of the grave or the Scale, considering them metaphorical.
Correction: Al-Ash'ari affirms them all as literal realities based on revelation, forming core Sunni creed.

Checkpoint
According to this passage, what is the condition for being brought out of Hellfire through intercession?
A) Only for those who never sinned.
B) For people who have been charred (امتحشوا).
C) Only for prophets.
D) For every Muslim automatically.

Key Takeaway
Imam al-Ash'ari affirms all major eschatological events as part of the Sunni creed, grounded in revelation.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
If the Scale were set before you right now, what one deed would you be most terrified to see exposed? What will you do today to address that fear?
?
The Prophet’s intercession is for those who believed but sinned. Are you living as if you will need that intercession, or do you assume you are safe? How does this affect your daily repentance?
?
You affirm the punishment of the grave. When was the last time you changed a habit because of that belief? If you have not, why does it remain a mere concept rather than a driving force?

The Dajjal and the Night Journey
Affirmation of Dajjal and Mi'raj as real events.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that Imam al-Ash'ari affirms the emergence of the Dajjal and the reality of the Prophet's Night Journey as core Sunni beliefs based on transmitted reports.

Core Idea
The Dajjal and the Mi'raj are literal events, not metaphors.

Imam al-Ash'ari continues his creedal exposition by affirming two major eschatological and miraculous events: the appearance of the Dajjal (Antichrist) as a major sign of the Hour, and the Prophet Muhammad's Night Journey (Isra and Mi'raj). These are matters of the unseen that must be accepted as true based on authentic narrations.

Guided Path
1
Read the Arabic affirmation
Read the two sentences in Arabic. Notice the verbs: 'nuqirru' (we affirm) and 'nusaddiqu' (we believe/affirm truth).
The author uses strong verbs of belief.
2
Understand the Dajjal
Reflect on the Dajjal as a major sign of the end times. The author ties his affirmation directly to the prophetic narration.
This counters those who deny the Dajjal.
3
Appreciate the Night Journey
The Mi'raj is affirmed as a physical, literal journey. This is a core Sunni belief against rationalist interpretations.
4
Connect to broader creed
Both beliefs are examples of sam'iyyat (matters known only through revelation). They underscore the principle of accepting what Allah and His Messenger have informed us.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
ونقر بخروج الدجال كما جاءت به الرواية عن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم. ونؤمن بعذاب القبر ونكير ومنكر ومسائلتهما المدفونين في قبورهم. ونصدق بحديث المعراج. ونصحح كثيراً من الرؤيا في المنام ونقر أن لذلك تفسيراً. ونرى الصدقة عن موتى المسلمين والدعاء لهم، ونؤمن أن الله ينفعهم بذلك. ونصدق بأن في الدنيا سحرة وسحراً، وأن السحر كائن موجود في الدنيا. وندين بالصلاة على من مات من أهل القبلة برهم وفاجرهم وتوراثهم. ونقر أن الجنة والنار مخلوقتان، وأن من مات وقتل فبأجله مات وقتل. وأن الأرزاق من قبل الله يرزقها عباده حلالاً وحراماً.
And we affirm the emergence of the Dajjal (Antichrist) as reported in the narration from the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him. And we believe in the punishment of the grave, and [the angels] Nakir and Munkar, and their questioning of the buried in their graves. And we affirm the truth of the hadith of the Night Journey (Mi'raj). And we confirm many of the visions in sleep, and we affirm that they have an interpretation. And we hold [the practice of] giving charity on behalf of dead Muslims and supplicating for them, and we believe that Allah benefits them thereby. And we affirm that in this world there are sorcerers and sorcery, and that sorcery exists and occurs in this world. And we make it our religion to pray [the funeral prayer] over those who die from the people of the qibla (i.e., Muslims), whether righteous or sinful, and [to affirm] their inheritance. And we affirm that Paradise and Hell are created, and that whoever dies or is killed [does so] by his appointed term, he dies or is killed. And that provisions are from Allah, He provides them to His servants, both lawful and unlawful.
Source pages p. 25

Commentary
This passage is a creedal summary of many eschatological and unseen matters: the Dajjal (a major sign of the Hour), the punishment of the grave and the questioning by Munkar and Nakir, the miraculous Night Journey of the Prophet, the validity of dream interpretation, the benefit of charity and supplication for the deceased, the reality of sorcery (refuting those who deny it), the obligation of funeral prayer for all Muslims regardless of sin, the creation of Paradise and Hell (refuting those who say they are not yet created), the predestination of lifespan (ajal), and that all provisions come from Allah even if unlawfully acquired (halal and haram). Each point defends Sunni orthodoxy against various sectarian deviations (Mu'tazila, Qadariyya, etc.).

Key Terms
الدجال
المعراج

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: That the Dajjal is merely a symbol or that the Night Journey was only a vision or dream.
Correction: Imam al-Ash'ari affirms both as literal events that occurred or will occur in space and time.

Checkpoint
According to the author, how is the belief in the Dajjal established?
A) Through rational proof
B) Through prophetic narration
C) Through consensus of scholars
D) Through personal experience

Key Takeaway
The Dajjal and the Night Journey are two real events that a Muslim must affirm based on prophetic reports.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
If the Dajjal is a real figure who will appear, and his deception is powerful, how are you actively preparing your heart and mind now to recognize and reject him when he comes? Commit to one concrete step this month to strengthen your knowledge of the prophetic warnings about him.
?
The lesson insists the Night Journey was a literal, physical event. Have you ever been tempted to reinterpret it metaphorically to make it more 'reasonable'? What does your willingness to accept it literally reveal about your trust in Allah's power and His Messenger's truthfulness? Resolve to affirm it without reservation.
?
Both the Dajjal and the Mi'raj are matters of the unseen that we accept on authority of revelation. Is there any teaching from the Quran or Sunnah that you struggle to accept because it seems too extraordinary? Pray for the strength to submit your intellect to revelation completely.

Punishment of the Grave and the Questioning Angels
Belief in the punishment of the grave and the questioning angels.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the Sunni belief in the punishment of the grave and the questioning by the angels Munkar and Nakir as part of the unseen realities of the barzakh.

Core Idea
The grave holds real punishment and questioning for the deceased.

Imam al-Ash'ari now turns to the intermediate realm (barzakh) between death and resurrection. He affirms both the punishment of the grave and the specific angels, Munkar and Nakir, who interrogate the dead. These beliefs are based on authentic hadiths and are central to Sunni eschatology.

Guided Path
1
Read the Arabic text
Notice the phrase 'nu'minu' (we believe) and the specifics: punishment of the grave, the two angels, and the questioning.
2
Understand the punishment of the grave
This is a physical and spiritual punishment experienced by the soul in the grave. It is real, though its nature is partly unknown.
This refutes those who deny any punishment before the Day of Judgment.
3
Learn about Munkar and Nakir
These two angels are sent to question every deceased person about their Lord, religion, and prophet. Their names are derived from hadith.
The belief in them is part of standard Sunni creed.
4
Reflect on the universal scope
The questioning applies to all buried individuals ('the buried in their graves'). It is a universal experience for both believers and disbelievers.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
ونقر بخروج الدجال كما جاءت به الرواية عن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم. ونؤمن بعذاب القبر ونكير ومنكر ومسائلتهما المدفونين في قبورهم. ونصدق بحديث المعراج. ونصحح كثيراً من الرؤيا في المنام ونقر أن لذلك تفسيراً. ونرى الصدقة عن موتى المسلمين والدعاء لهم، ونؤمن أن الله ينفعهم بذلك. ونصدق بأن في الدنيا سحرة وسحراً، وأن السحر كائن موجود في الدنيا. وندين بالصلاة على من مات من أهل القبلة برهم وفاجرهم وتوراثهم. ونقر أن الجنة والنار مخلوقتان، وأن من مات وقتل فبأجله مات وقتل. وأن الأرزاق من قبل الله يرزقها عباده حلالاً وحراماً.
And we affirm the emergence of the Dajjal (Antichrist) as reported in the narration from the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him. And we believe in the punishment of the grave, and [the angels] Nakir and Munkar, and their questioning of the buried in their graves. And we affirm the truth of the hadith of the Night Journey (Mi'raj). And we confirm many of the visions in sleep, and we affirm that they have an interpretation. And we hold [the practice of] giving charity on behalf of dead Muslims and supplicating for them, and we believe that Allah benefits them thereby. And we affirm that in this world there are sorcerers and sorcery, and that sorcery exists and occurs in this world. And we make it our religion to pray [the funeral prayer] over those who die from the people of the qibla (i.e., Muslims), whether righteous or sinful, and [to affirm] their inheritance. And we affirm that Paradise and Hell are created, and that whoever dies or is killed [does so] by his appointed term, he dies or is killed. And that provisions are from Allah, He provides them to His servants, both lawful and unlawful.
Source pages p. 25

Commentary
This passage is a creedal summary of many eschatological and unseen matters: the Dajjal (a major sign of the Hour), the punishment of the grave and the questioning by Munkar and Nakir, the miraculous Night Journey of the Prophet, the validity of dream interpretation, the benefit of charity and supplication for the deceased, the reality of sorcery (refuting those who deny it), the obligation of funeral prayer for all Muslims regardless of sin, the creation of Paradise and Hell (refuting those who say they are not yet created), the predestination of lifespan (ajal), and that all provisions come from Allah even if unlawfully acquired (halal and haram). Each point defends Sunni orthodoxy against various sectarian deviations (Mu'tazila, Qadariyya, etc.).

Key Terms
عذاب القبر
نكير ومنكر

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: That the punishment of the grave is metaphorical or does not occur until the Day of Judgment.
Correction: Imam al-Ash'ari affirms it as a real punishment happening in the grave itself.

Checkpoint
What do the angels Munkar and Nakir do according to this passage?
A) They guard the gates of Paradise
B) They question the buried in their graves
C) They record deeds
D) They blow the Trumpet

Key Takeaway
The punishment of the grave and the questioning by Munkar and Nakir are real events in the unseen realm of the barzakh.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
You will soon be in your grave, being questioned by Munkar and Nakir about your Lord, your religion, and your prophet. Right now, can you say with certainty that you know the answers? If not, what specific step will you take today to learn them and commit them to heart?
?
The punishment of the grave is a terrifying reality. Do you live as if you truly believe that your actions today will determine your experience in the grave? Name one sin you have been clinging to that you know you must abandon for fear of that punishment.
?
Imagine yourself buried, alone, waiting for the first question. Are you confident that your faith is strong enough to answer correctly? What will you change in your daily worship to ensure that your heart is firmly anchored to the truth when that moment comes?

Dreams, Charity for the Deceased, and Sorcery
Affirmation of dream interpretation, charity for deceased, and sorcery.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that Imam al-Ash'ari affirms the validity of dream interpretation, the benefit of charity for the dead, and the reality of sorcery in this world.

Core Idea
Dreams have meaning, charity benefits the deceased, and sorcery is real.

In this passage, Imam al-Ash'ari addresses three distinct but related matters of the unseen: the interpretation of dreams, the efficacy of charity and supplication for the deceased, and the existence of sorcery. Each counters specific sectarian denials or innovations.

Guided Path
1
Read the three affirmations
Read the Arabic sentences. Note the verbs: nusaddiqu, nara, nusaddiqu—all indicate belief or practice.
2
Understand dream interpretation
The author affirms that many dreams have true interpretations, legitimizing the practice of dream interpretation within limits.
This opposes those who dismiss all dreams.
3
Learn about charity for the dead
The author explicitly states that charity and supplication benefit the deceased, a common Sunni practice that was disputed by some sects.
The phrase 'yunfa'uhum' indicates real benefit.
4
Acknowledge the reality of sorcery
The author affirms that sorcerers and sorcery exist and occur, refuting those who deny its possibility or accuse others of disbelief for believing in it.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
ونقر بخروج الدجال كما جاءت به الرواية عن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم. ونؤمن بعذاب القبر ونكير ومنكر ومسائلتهما المدفونين في قبورهم. ونصدق بحديث المعراج. ونصحح كثيراً من الرؤيا في المنام ونقر أن لذلك تفسيراً. ونرى الصدقة عن موتى المسلمين والدعاء لهم، ونؤمن أن الله ينفعهم بذلك. ونصدق بأن في الدنيا سحرة وسحراً، وأن السحر كائن موجود في الدنيا. وندين بالصلاة على من مات من أهل القبلة برهم وفاجرهم وتوراثهم. ونقر أن الجنة والنار مخلوقتان، وأن من مات وقتل فبأجله مات وقتل. وأن الأرزاق من قبل الله يرزقها عباده حلالاً وحراماً.
And we affirm the emergence of the Dajjal (Antichrist) as reported in the narration from the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him. And we believe in the punishment of the grave, and [the angels] Nakir and Munkar, and their questioning of the buried in their graves. And we affirm the truth of the hadith of the Night Journey (Mi'raj). And we confirm many of the visions in sleep, and we affirm that they have an interpretation. And we hold [the practice of] giving charity on behalf of dead Muslims and supplicating for them, and we believe that Allah benefits them thereby. And we affirm that in this world there are sorcerers and sorcery, and that sorcery exists and occurs in this world. And we make it our religion to pray [the funeral prayer] over those who die from the people of the qibla (i.e., Muslims), whether righteous or sinful, and [to affirm] their inheritance. And we affirm that Paradise and Hell are created, and that whoever dies or is killed [does so] by his appointed term, he dies or is killed. And that provisions are from Allah, He provides them to His servants, both lawful and unlawful.

Commentary
This passage is a creedal summary of many eschatological and unseen matters: the Dajjal (a major sign of the Hour), the punishment of the grave and the questioning by Munkar and Nakir, the miraculous Night Journey of the Prophet, the validity of dream interpretation, the benefit of charity and supplication for the deceased, the reality of sorcery (refuting those who deny it), the obligation of funeral prayer for all Muslims regardless of sin, the creation of Paradise and Hell (refuting those who say they are not yet created), the predestination of lifespan (ajal), and that all provisions come from Allah even if unlawfully acquired (halal and haram). Each point defends Sunni orthodoxy against various sectarian deviations (Mu'tazila, Qadariyya, etc.).

Key Terms

الصدقة
السحر

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: That the dead cannot benefit from any actions of the living, or that sorcery does not exist.
Correction: Imam al-Ash'ari affirms both: charity benefits the dead, and sorcery is real.

Checkpoint
According to the author, who benefits from charity given on behalf of deceased Muslims?
A) Only the giver
B) The deceased, by Allah's will
C) Only the poor recipients
D) No one, as the dead cannot benefit

Key Takeaway
Dreams have meaningful interpretations, charity helps the deceased, and sorcery is a real phenomenon in the world.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
Do you take your dreams seriously? Have you ever dismissed a dream as meaningless, ignoring that they can be signs or warnings? How will you pay more attention to your dreams starting tonight?
?
When you give charity, do you ever intend for it to benefit your deceased loved ones? If not, why? What holds you back from believing that Allah can benefit them through your sadaqa?
?
Sorcery is real. Have you ever sought protection? Do you truly trust Allah's protection against sorcery, or do you sometimes mock such beliefs as superstition? Examine your heart: do you fully affirm the reality of the unseen?

Funeral Prayer, Creation of Paradise and Hell, and Predestination
Affirmation of funeral prayer, createdness of Paradise and Hell, and predestination.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the Sunni positions on funeral prayer for all Muslims, the createdness of Paradise and Hell, and the predestination of lifespans and provisions.

Core Idea
Paradise and Hell are created; lifespans and provisions are predestined by Allah.

Imam al-Ash'ari concludes this section by affirming several creedal points related to eschatology and divine decree. He establishes the obligation of funeral prayer for all Muslims regardless of sin, the pre-existence of Paradise and Hell, and that every person's death (whether natural or violent) occurs by a predetermined term, and all provisions come from Allah—whether lawful or unlawful.

Guided Path
1
Read the Arabic passage
Notice the three main affirmations: funeral prayer, creation of Jannah and Jahannam, and predestination (ajal and rizq).
2
Understand funeral prayer for all Muslims
The author says 'we make it our religion' to pray over every Muslim, righteous or sinful. This emphasizes community unity and the permissibility of praying for sinners.
This refutes those who refuse to pray for sinners.
3
Grasp the creation of Paradise and Hell
Paradise and Hell are already created and exist now; they are not merely future states. This counters the Mu'tazila view that they will be created later.
4
Accept predestination of lifespan and provision
The author affirms that every death occurs by a fixed term (ajal), and all provisions come from Allah—whether halal or haram. This upholds divine sovereignty over all events.
5
Reflect on the implications
These beliefs reinforce trust in Allah's decree and encourage prayers for all Muslims.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
ونقر بخروج الدجال كما جاءت به الرواية عن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم. ونؤمن بعذاب القبر ونكير ومنكر ومسائلتهما المدفونين في قبورهم. ونصدق بحديث المعراج. ونصحح كثيراً من الرؤيا في المنام ونقر أن لذلك تفسيراً. ونرى الصدقة عن موتى المسلمين والدعاء لهم، ونؤمن أن الله ينفعهم بذلك. ونصدق بأن في الدنيا سحرة وسحراً، وأن السحر كائن موجود في الدنيا. وندين بالصلاة على من مات من أهل القبلة برهم وفاجرهم وتوراثهم. ونقر أن الجنة والنار مخلوقتان، وأن من مات وقتل فبأجله مات وقتل. وأن الأرزاق من قبل الله يرزقها عباده حلالاً وحراماً.
And we affirm the emergence of the Dajjal (Antichrist) as reported in the narration from the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him. And we believe in the punishment of the grave, and [the angels] Nakir and Munkar, and their questioning of the buried in their graves. And we affirm the truth of the hadith of the Night Journey (Mi'raj). And we confirm many of the visions in sleep, and we affirm that they have an interpretation. And we hold [the practice of] giving charity on behalf of dead Muslims and supplicating for them, and we believe that Allah benefits them thereby. And we affirm that in this world there are sorcerers and sorcery, and that sorcery exists and occurs in this world. And we make it our religion to pray [the funeral prayer] over those who die from the people of the qibla (i.e., Muslims), whether righteous or sinful, and [to affirm] their inheritance. And we affirm that Paradise and Hell are created, and that whoever dies or is killed [does so] by his appointed term, he dies or is killed. And that provisions are from Allah, He provides them to His servants, both lawful and unlawful.
Source pages p. 25

Commentary
This passage is a creedal summary of many eschatological and unseen matters: the Dajjal (a major sign of the Hour), the punishment of the grave and the questioning by Munkar and Nakir, the miraculous Night Journey of the Prophet, the validity of dream interpretation, the benefit of charity and supplication for the deceased, the reality of sorcery (refuting those who deny it), the obligation of funeral prayer for all Muslims regardless of sin, the creation of Paradise and Hell (refuting those who say they are not yet created), the predestination of lifespan (ajal), and that all provisions come from Allah even if unlawfully acquired (halal and haram). Each point defends Sunni orthodoxy against various sectarian deviations (Mu'tazila, Qadariyya, etc.).

Key Terms
الصلاة على الميت
الجنة والنار مخلوقتان
الأجل
الأرزاق

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: That Paradise and Hell are not yet created, or that humans cause their own death, or that unlawful provision is not from Allah.
Correction: Imam al-Ash'ari affirms creation of both realms, fixed lifespans, and divine origin of all provision.

Checkpoint
What does the author say about the provision (rizq) that is unlawful (haram)?
A) It is not from Allah
B) It is also provided by Allah
C) It does not exist
D) It is from Satan

Key Takeaway
Sunni doctrine holds that Paradise and Hell are created, lifespans are fixed, and all provision—lawful or not—comes from Allah.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
Do you truly believe that even the haram wealth you have ever touched was from Allah? How does that change your repentance and your view of your own sins?
?
You have likely attended funerals of sinners. Did your heart consider them less deserving of prayer? The source says to pray over all Muslims. How will you correct that hidden judgment starting today?
?
The lesson says your death is fixed. Does that weaken or strengthen your urgency to obey Allah? Be honest — what excuses have you made because you thought you had more time?

Satan's Whisper Refutes Mu'tazila
Affirms Satan's whisper and refutes Mu'tazila/Jahmiyya with Quran.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that Satan can whisper and affect humans, contrary to the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya, based on Quranic evidence.

Core Idea
Satan's whisper is real; Mu'tazila denied it to protect free will.

The author begins by affirming a fundamental Sunni belief: Satan has real influence on humans through whispering and confusion. This directly contradicts the rationalist sects who argued that such influence would compromise human accountability. Two Quranic verses are cited as proof.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage and translation
Read the Arabic passage once, then the literal translation. Notice how the author states the belief and then immediately provides Quranic support.
The passage uses two verses: one about usury and one from Surah an-Nas.
2
Identify the opposing view
The author explicitly names the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya as opponents. Think about why they denied Satan's influence.
They believed it would limit human free will.
3
Analyze the Quranic evidence
Look at the two verses: the first shows physical confusion from Satan, the second shows whispering in the heart. Both affirm different types of influence.
4
Reflect on the theological implication
Consider how this belief does not negate human responsibility, as Satan's influence is within God's decree and humans still choose.
This is a crucial point in Sunni kalam.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
وأن الشيطان يوسوس للإنسان ويسلكه ويتخبطه خلافاً لقول المعتزلة والجهمية، كما قال الله عز وجل: {الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبَا لَا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَقُومُ الَّذِي يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ مِنَ الْمَسِّ} وكما قال: {مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ * الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ}. ونقول: إن الصالحين يجوز أن يخصهم الله عز وجل بآيات يظهرها عليهم. وقولنا في أطفال المشركين: إن الله يؤجج لهم في الآخرة ناراً ثم يقول لهم اقتحموها، كما جاءت بذلك الرواية.
And that Satan whispers to man, and enters into him, and confounds him, contrary to the saying of the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya, as Allah, Mighty and Majestic, said: 'Those who consume usury will not stand except as one stands whom Satan has confounded by [his] touch,' and as He said: 'From the evil of the whisperer who withdraws, who whispers into the hearts of mankind, from among the jinn and mankind.' And we say: Indeed, the righteous ones may be singled out by Allah, Mighty and Majestic, with miracles (ayat) that He manifests through them. And our saying concerning the children of polytheists: Allah will kindle for them a fire in the Hereafter, then say to them: 'Leap into it,' as the narration has reported.
Source pages p. 25

Commentary
This passage addresses three topics. First, it affirms the reality of Satan's whispers and his influence on humans, refuting the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya who denied such influence (to protect free will). Two Quranic verses are cited: the description of usurers being confounded by Satan, and Surah an-Nas about whispering. Second, it affirms the possibility of karamat (miracles) for righteous saints, a belief denied by some sects like the Mu'tazila. Third, it addresses the fate of children of polytheists: they will be tested in the afterlife with a fire, and those who obey the command to jump in will enter Paradise, as per a hadith. This refutes both those who consign them all to Hell and those who say they will not be judged.

Key Terms
وسوسة الشيطان
المعتزلة والجهمية

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some claim Satan has no power to influence humans at all.
Correction: Sunni Islam affirms that Satan can whisper and cause confusion, but this is within God's decree and does not negate free will.

Checkpoint
Which two sects are refuted in this passage regarding Satan's influence?
A) Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya
B) Ash'aris and Maturidis
C) Shi'a and Khawarij
D) Sufis and Hanbalis

Key Takeaway
Satan's whisper and confusion are real, confirmed by the Quran, and the Mu'tazila/Jahmiyya denial is rejected.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
Have you ever dismissed the evil whispers in your heart as just your own thoughts, rather than recognizing them as the work of Satan? How will you now actively discern the difference based on what this lesson teaches about the reality of his whisper? Commit to a specific practice, such as seeking refuge in Allah whenever you feel a whisper, and journaling about it this week.
?
The Mu'tazila denied Satan's whisper to protect human free will. Do you ever deny spiritual influences in your life to maintain a false sense of control? What would it mean for you to humbly admit that Satan can whisper to you, and that you need Allah's protection? Name one area of your life where you have been relying on your own strength, and resolve to recite the two last surahs of the Quran daily for the next month.
?
If Satan can confound a person as the verse on usury shows, what does that reveal about the spiritual state of someone who persists in sin without seeking forgiveness? How does this lesson compel you to examine your own heart for the effects of unchecked sin? Choose one sin you have been minimizing, and today, repent sincerely and ask Allah to protect you from Satan’s confusion over it.

Miracles of the Righteous Saints
Affirms the possibility of miracles for righteous saints.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that Allah may grant extraordinary signs to righteous saints, affirming the possibility of karamat.

Core Idea
Allah can honor saints with miracles; this is not contrary to prophethood.

After discussing Satan's influence, the author briefly affirms another important Sunni belief: the possibility of miraculous events (karamat) being manifested through righteous saints. This refutes those who restricted miracles to prophets alone.

Guided Path
1
Read the short passage
Read the Arabic sentence and its translation. Notice the concise wording: 'we say' indicates a creedal statement.
2
Identify the key term
The word 'آيات' here means signs/miracles (karamat). Understand that these are distinct from prophetic miracles (mu'jizat).
Karamat are gifts from Allah, not proof of prophethood.
3
Consider the implication
Why would some sects deny saintly miracles? How does affirming them strengthen the faith of believers?
Deniers often argued that miracles after the Prophet would confuse the finality of prophethood.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
وأن الشيطان يوسوس للإنسان ويسلكه ويتخبطه خلافاً لقول المعتزلة والجهمية، كما قال الله عز وجل: {الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبَا لَا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَقُومُ الَّذِي يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ مِنَ الْمَسِّ} وكما قال: {مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ * الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ}. ونقول: إن الصالحين يجوز أن يخصهم الله عز وجل بآيات يظهرها عليهم. وقولنا في أطفال المشركين: إن الله يؤجج لهم في الآخرة ناراً ثم يقول لهم اقتحموها، كما جاءت بذلك الرواية.
And that Satan whispers to man, and enters into him, and confounds him, contrary to the saying of the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya, as Allah, Mighty and Majestic, said: 'Those who consume usury will not stand except as one stands whom Satan has confounded by [his] touch,' and as He said: 'From the evil of the whisperer who withdraws, who whispers into the hearts of mankind, from among the jinn and mankind.' And we say: Indeed, the righteous ones may be singled out by Allah, Mighty and Majestic, with miracles (ayat) that He manifests through them. This refutes both those who consign them all to Hell and those who say they will not be judged.

Key Terms

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Only prophets can perform miracles; saints cannot.
Correction: Sunni belief holds that Allah can grant miracles to righteous saints as a sign of their piety and as a blessing.

Checkpoint
What does the author say about miracles of the righteous?
A) They are impossible
B) They are possible when Allah wills
C) They are obligatory for every saint
D) They only happened in the past

Key Takeaway
Allah may grant miracles (karamat) to righteous saints as a special favor.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
Do you truly believe that Allah can honor ordinary believers like you with miracles? If so, how does that change your view of the righteous people you know—or your own potential for nearness to Him?
?
If miracles (karamat) are a sign of Allah's pleasure, what does that say about the state of your own heart and actions? Are you living in a way that seeks His pleasure, or are you content with mediocrity?
?
The passage says Allah 'singles out' the righteous for miracles. Does that inspire you to strive for righteousness, or do you dismiss such possibilities as ancient stories? What would have to change in your life for you to be among those whom Allah singles out?

Children of Polytheists Tested in Afterlife
States the test of polytheist children with a fire in the afterlife.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the Sunni position that children of polytheists will be tested with a fire on the Day of Judgment, based on a narration.

Core Idea
Polytheist children are not automatically damned; they undergo a test in the afterlife.

The final topic in this passage addresses a much-debated question: what happens to children who died before reaching maturity and whose parents were polytheists? The author states a clear position based on a transmitted narration (riwaya).

Guided Path
1
Read the passage carefully
Read the Arabic and translation. Note the phrase 'كما جاءت بذلك الرواية' indicating reliance on a specific narration.
The narration is likely a hadith about the testing of children in the afterlife.
2
Understand the test
The test involves a fire: Allah will kindle a fire and command them to jump in. This is a test of obedience, not punishment.
Those who obey enter Paradise, those who disobey enter Hell.
3
Contrast with other views
Some groups said all such children go to Hell, others said they go to Paradise. The Sunni position is a middle path: they are tested.
This reflects Allah's justice, as children are not accountable before maturity.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
وأن الشيطان يوسوس للإنسان ويسلكه ويتخبطه خلافاً لقول المعتزلة والجهمية، كما قال الله عز وجل: {الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبَا لَا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَقُومُ الَّذِي يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ مِنَ الْمَسِّ} وكما قال: {مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ * الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ}. ونقول: إن الصالحين يجوز أن يخصهم الله عز وجل بآيات يظهرها عليهم. وقولنا في أطفال المشركين: إن الله يؤجج لهم في الآخرة ناراً ثم يقول لهم اقتحموها، كما جاءت بذلك الرواية.
And that Satan whispers to man, and enters into him, and confounds him, contrary to the saying of the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya, as Allah, Mighty and Majestic, said: 'Those who consume usury will not stand except as one stands whom Satan has confounded by [his] touch,' and as He said: 'From the evil of the whisperer who withdraws, who whispers into the hearts of mankind, from among the jinn and mankind.' And we say: Indeed, the righteous ones may be singled out by Allah, Mighty and Majestic, with miracles (ayat) that He manifests through them. And our saying concerning the children of polytheists: Allah will kindle for them a fire in the Hereafter, then say to them: 'Leap into it,' as the narration has reported.
Source pages p. 25

Commentary
This passage addresses three topics. First, it affirms the reality of Satan's whispers and his influence on humans, refuting the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya who denied such influence (to protect free will). Two Quranic verses are cited: the description of usurers being confounded by Satan, and Surah an-Nas about whispering. Second, it affirms the possibility of karamat (miracles) for righteous saints, a belief denied by some sects like the Mu'tazila. Third, it addresses the fate of children of polytheists: they will be tested in the afterlife with a fire, and those who obey the command to jump in will enter Paradise, as per a hadith. This refutes both those who consign them all to Hell and those who say they will not be judged.

Key Terms
أطفال المشركين

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Children of polytheists automatically go to Hell or Paradise without test.
Correction: Sunni belief holds they are tested in the afterlife with a fire; their choice determines their fate.

Checkpoint
What does the author say Allah will do to the children of polytheists on the Day of Judgment?
A) Send them directly to Hell
B) Send them directly to Paradise
C) Kindle a fire and command them to leap into it
D) Leave them in a state of limbo

Key Takeaway
Children of polytheists will be tested with a fire in the afterlife, and their obedience or disobedience determines their final abode.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
If Allah tests even the children of polytheists with a fire, what does that say about the importance of immediate obedience from your own heart? Are you quick to leap when Allah commands, or do you hesitate and rationalize?
?
The test reveals the true state of their submission. How would you fare if you were given a similar test today—would your obedience be instinctive or reluctant?
?
This teaching shows that Allah gives every soul a fair chance. Have you ever judged someone's fate prematurely, assuming they are beyond Allah's mercy? How must you change your view of others after learning this?

beginner

Creed: Wiping over Socks and Obedience to Imams
Lists first two creedal articles: wiping over socks and obedience to imams.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the Sunni position on wiping over leather socks as a sunna and the obligation to obey Muslim imams while rejecting rebellion.

Core Idea
Sunni creed affirms ritual practice and political obedience, rejecting rebellion even against unjust rulers.

Imam al-Ash'ari begins his summary of Sunni creed with practical matters: the sunna of wiping over socks and the duty towards Muslim leaders. These articles establish the balanced Sunni approach—committed to both ritual precision and political stability.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage
Read the Arabic passage once, then the translation. Notice the concise style of declaring creedal positions.
2
Identify the two main topics
The passage covers two distinct articles: wiping over socks and the stance on imams and rebellion. Identify each and see how they are linked.
The second part includes supplication for imams, acknowledging their leadership, and condemning rebellion.
3
Consider the opposing views
The passage explicitly contrasts with those who deny the sunna of wiping, and those who justify rebellion. Reflect on how this defines Sunni identity.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
وأن المسح على الخفين سنة في الحضر والسفر ، خلافاً لمن أنكر ذلك ، ونرى الدعاء لأئمة المسلمين بالصلاح والإقرار بإمامتهم وتضليل من رأى الخروج عليهم إذا ظهر منهم ترك الاستقامة . وندين بإنكار الخروج بالسيف وترك القتال في الفتنة ، ونقر بخروج الدجال كما جاءت به الرواية عن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم. ونؤمن بعذاب القبر ونكير ومنكر ومسائلتهما المدفونين بقبورهم ونصدق بحديث المعراج . ونصحح كثيراً من الرؤيا في المنام ونقر أن لذلك تفسيراً . ونرى الصدقة عن موتى المسلمين والدعاء لهم : ونؤمن أن الله ينفعهم بذلك ، ونصدق بأن في الدنيا سحرة وسحراً ، وأن السحر كائن موجود في الدنيا . وندين بالصلاة على من مات من أهل القبلة برهم وفاجرهم وتوراثهم . ونقر أن الجنة والنار مخلوقتان ، وأن من مات وقتل فبأجله مات وقتل وأن الأرزاق من قبل الله يرزقها عباده حلالاً وحراماً ، وأن الشيطان يوسوس للإنسان ويسلكه ويتخبطه خلافاً لقول المعتزلة والجهمية ، كما قال الله عز وجل : ( الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبوا لَا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَقُومُ الَّذِى يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ مِنَ المس ) وكما قال : ( مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ * الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ الناس مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ . ونقول : إن الصالحين يجوز أن يخصهم الله عز وجل بآيات يظهرها عليهم وقولنا في أطفال المشركين : إن الله يؤجج لهم في الآخرة ناراً ثم يقول لهم اقتحموها ، كما جاءت بذلك الرواية . وندين الله عز وجل بأنه يعلم ما العباد عاملون وإلى ما هم صائرون ، وما كان وما يكون وما لا يكون أن لو كان كيف كان يكون وبطاعة الأئمة المسلمين، ونرى مفارقة كل داعية إلى بدعته ومجانبة أهل الهوى . انتهى بحروفه
And that wiping over the leather socks (al-mash 'ala al-khuffayn) is a sunna in residence and travel, contrary to those who deny that. And we hold that supplication for the Muslim imams for righteousness and acknowledgment of their leadership, and misguidance of those who see rebellion against them when they manifest abandonment of uprightness. And we profess rejection of rebellion with the sword and abandonment of fighting in turmoil (al-fitna), and we affirm the emergence of the Dajjal as the narration from the Messenger of God (peace be upon him) has come. And we believe in the punishment of the grave, and Nakir and Munkar, and their questioning of the buried in their graves, and we affirm the narration of the Night Journey (al-mi'raj). And we consider many dreams in sleep as true and affirm that they have an interpretation. And we hold that charity for the deceased Muslims and supplication for them—and we believe that God benefits them by that—and we affirm that in the world there are sorcerers and sorcery, and that sorcery exists in the world. And we profess prayer over whoever dies from the people of the qibla, their righteous and their sinful, and their inheritance [among each other]. And we affirm that Paradise and Hell are created, and that whoever dies or is killed dies and is killed by his appointed term, and that provisions are from God, He provides them to His servants, lawful and unlawful, and that Satan whispers to man and leads him and confounds him, contrary to the claim of the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya, as God Almighty said: (Those who consume interest will not rise except as one rises whom Satan has confounded by touch) and as He said: (From the evil of the whisperer who withdraws, who whispers in the breasts of people, from among the jinn and people). And we say: It is permissible for God Almighty to distinguish the righteous with signs that He manifests upon them. And our statement concerning the children of polytheists: that God will kindle for them a fire in the Hereafter, then say to them: Leap into it, as the narration has come. And we profess to God Almighty that He knows what the servants are doing and to what they are progressing, and what was and what will be and what would not be if it were to be, how it would be. And [we profess] obedience to the Muslim imams. And we hold that we should separate from every caller to his innovation and shun the people of desires. It is concluded verbatim.
Source pages p. 26

Commentary
This passage is a comprehensive list of creedal articles that form the core of Ahl al-Sunnah according to Imam al-Ash'ari. It begins by affirming the sunna of wiping over leather socks during travel and residence, countering those who reject it. It then addresses political obedience: supplication for Muslim rulers, acknowledgment of their legitimacy, and condemnation of rebellion even if they deviate. Rebellion with the sword is rejected, and fighting during fitna is abandoned. The passage then moves to eschatological beliefs: the emergence of the Dajjal, punishment of the grave, the angels Nakir and Munkar questioning the dead, and the truth of the Night Journey (Isra and Mi'raj). Dreams are affirmed as having true interpretations. Charity for deceased Muslims is beneficial, and sorcery exists as a reality in the world. Prayer is offered over all Muslims of the qibla regardless of righteousness, and inheritance is recognized. Paradise and Hell are already created. Death occurs by appointed term, and provision is from God, both lawful and unlawful. Satan's whispering and influence on humans is affirmed, supported by Quranic verses against the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya who deny this. The possibility of miracles for saints is affirmed. The fate of polytheists' children is specified as a test with fire in the afterlife. God's knowledge encompasses all—past, present, future, and even hypotheticals. Obedience to Muslim imams is required. Finally, separation from innovators and people of desires is mandated. The passage ends with 'concluded verbatim' marking the end of the creed.

Key Terms
المسح على الخفين
أئمة المسلمين
الخروج بالسيف
الفتنة

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some believe that rebellion against unjust rulers is always required.
Correction: Sunni creed holds that rebellion is prohibited and that even rulers who abandon uprightness should not be fought, unless they commit clear disbelief.

Checkpoint
What does the text say about rebellion against imams?
A) It is obligatory if they are unjust.
B) It is permissible with conditions.
C) It is rejected and those who advocate it are misguided.
D) It is recommended to establish justice.

Key Takeaway
The first two articles of the Sunni creed affirm ritual practice (wiping over socks) and political obedience, rejecting rebellion.

Unseen Matters

Creed: Eschatological Beliefs
Lists eschatological and supernatural creedal articles.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the Sunni creed concerning the Dajjal, punishment of the grave, the Night Journey, dreams, charity for the deceased, and the reality of sorcery.

Core Idea
Sunni creed affirms unseen realities: Dajjal, grave punishment, al-Mi'raj, true dreams, benefit of charity for dead, and sorcery.

After political articles, Imam al-Ash'ari turns to eschatological and supernatural matters. These beliefs define the Sunni position on what happens after death and before the Day of Judgment, including details of the grave, the Dajjal, and the reality of sorcery.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage
Read the Arabic passage and translation. Note that each sentence introduces a distinct belief.
2
List the beliefs mentioned
Identify each creedal point: Dajjal, punishment of the grave, Nakir and Munkar, al-Mi'raj, dreams, charity for deceased, sorcery.
There are seven items in this segment.
3
Understand the rationale
Why does Imam al-Ash'ari include these? They counter groups like Mu'tazila who denied some of these (e.g., punishment of grave, sorcery).

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
وأن المسح على الخفين سنة في الحضر والسفر ، خلافاً لمن أنكر ذلك ، ونرى الدعاء لأئمة المسلمين بالصلاح والإقرار بإمامتهم وتضليل من رأى الخروج عليهم إذا ظهر منهم ترك الاستقامة . وندين بإنكار الخروج بالسيف وترك القتال في الفتنة ، ونقر بخروج الدجال كما جاءت به الرواية عن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم. ونؤمن بعذاب القبر ونكير ومنكر ومسائلتهما المدفونين بقبورهم ونصدق بحديث المعراج . ونصحح كثيراً من الرؤيا في المنام ونقر أن لذلك تفسيراً . ونرى الصدقة عن موتى المسلمين والدعاء لهم : ونؤمن أن الله ينفعهم بذلك ، ونصدق بأن في الدنيا سحرة وسحراً ، وأن السحر كائن موجود في الدنيا . وندين بالصلاة على من مات من أهل القبلة برهم وفاجرهم وتوراثهم . ونقر أن الجنة والنار مخلوقتان ، وأن من مات وقتل فبأجله مات وقتل وأن الأرزاق من قبل الله يرزقها عباده حلالاً وحراماً ، وأن الشيطان يوسوس للإنسان ويسلكه ويتخبطه خلافاً لقول المعتزلة والجهمية ، كما قال الله عز وجل : ( الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبوا لَا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَقُومُ الَّذِى يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ مِنَ المس ) وكما قال : ( مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ * الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ الناس مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ . ونقول : إن الصالحين يجوز أن يخصهم الله عز وجل بآيات يظهرها عليهم وقولنا في أطفال المشركين : إن الله يؤجج لهم في الآخرة ناراً ثم يقول لهم اقتحموها ، كما جاءت بذلك الرواية . وندين الله عز وجل بأنه يعلم ما العباد عاملون وإلى ما هم صائرون ، وما كان وما يكون وما لا يكون أن لو كان كيف كان يكون وبطاعة الأئمة المسلمين، ونرى مفارقة كل داعية إلى بدعته ومجانبة أهل الهوى . انتهى بحروفه
And that wiping over the leather socks (al-mash 'ala al-khuffayn) is a sunna in residence and travel, contrary to those who deny that. And we hold that supplication for the Muslim imams for righteousness and acknowledgment of their leadership, and misguidance of those who see rebellion against them when they manifest abandonment of uprightness. And we profess rejection of rebellion with the sword and abandonment of fighting in turmoil (al-fitna), and we affirm the emergence of the Dajjal as the narration from the Messenger of God (peace be upon him) has come. And we believe in the punishment of the grave, and Nakir and Munkar, and their questioning of the buried in their graves, and we affirm the narration of the Night Journey (al-mi'raj). And we consider many dreams in sleep as true and affirm that they have an interpretation. And we hold that charity for the deceased Muslims and supplication for them—and we believe that God benefits them by that—and we affirm that in the world there are sorcerers and sorcery, and that sorcery exists in the world. And we profess prayer over whoever dies from the people of the qibla, their righteous and their sinful, and their inheritance [among each other]. And we affirm that Paradise and Hell are created, and that whoever dies or is killed dies and is killed by his appointed term, and that provisions are from God, He provides them to His servants, lawful and unlawful, and that Satan whispers to man and leads him and confounds him, contrary to the claim of the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya, as God Almighty said: (Those who consume interest will not rise except as one rises whom Satan has confounded by touch) and as He said: (From the evil of the whisperer who withdraws, who whispers in the breasts of people, from among the jinn and people). And we say: It is permissible for God Almighty to distinguish the righteous with signs that He manifests upon them. And our statement concerning the children of polytheists: that God will kindle for them a fire in the Hereafter, then say to them: Leap into it, as the narration has come. And we profess to God Almighty that He knows what the servants are doing and to what they are progressing, and what was and what will be and what would not be if it were to be, how it would be. And [we profess] obedience to the Muslim imams. And we hold that we should separate from every caller to his innovation and shun the people of desires. It is concluded verbatim.
Source pages p. 26

Commentary
This passage is a comprehensive list of creedal articles that form the core of Ahl al-Sunnah according to Imam al-Ash'ari. It begins by affirming the sunna of wiping over leather socks during travel and residence, countering those who reject it. It then addresses political obedience: supplication for Muslim rulers, acknowledgment of their legitimacy, and condemnation of rebellion even if they deviate. Rebellion with the sword is rejected, and fighting during fitna is abandoned. The passage then moves to eschatological beliefs: the emergence of the Dajjal, punishment of the grave, the angels Nakir and Munkar questioning the dead, and the truth of the Night Journey (Isra and Mi'raj). Dreams are affirmed as having true interpretations. Charity for deceased Muslims is beneficial, and sorcery exists as a reality in the world. Prayer is offered over all Muslims of the qibla regardless of righteousness, and inheritance is recognized. Paradise and Hell are already created. Death occurs by appointed term, and provision is from God, both lawful and unlawful. Satan's whispering and influence on humans is affirmed, supported by Quranic verses against the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya who deny this. The possibility of miracles for saints is affirmed. The fate of polytheists' children is specified as a test with fire in the afterlife. God's knowledge encompasses all—past, present, future, and even hypotheticals. Obedience to Muslim imams is required. Finally, separation from innovators and people of desires is mandated. The passage ends with 'concluded verbatim' marking the end of the creed.

Key Terms
الدجال
عذاب القبر
نكير ومنكر
المعراج
الرؤيا
السحر

Key Terms from Source Passage

Checkpoint
According to this passage, what is the ruling on the Night Journey (al-mi'raj)?
A) It is a metaphorical story.
B) It is a true event affirmed by narrations.
C) It is a dream of the Prophet.
D) It is not discussed.

Key Takeaway
Sunni creed affirms unseen realities including Dajjal, grave punishment, Mi'raj, true dreams, benefit of charity for dead, and reality of sorcery.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
Does the certainty of grave punishment and questioning change how you prepare for your own death? What concrete steps will you take this week to act on that awareness?
?
Have you ever dismissed a vivid dream as meaningless when it might have carried a true sign from Allah? After this lesson, how will you treat your dreams going forward?
?
If you truly believe that charity and du'a benefit the deceased, how often do you actively make du'a for your loved ones who have passed? What will you start doing today?

Creed: Afterlife, Predestination, and Satan's Role
Lists creedal articles on prayer for deceased, Paradise and Hell, predestination, provision, and Satan's whispering.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the Sunni creed on prayer for all Muslims, the createdness of Paradise and Hell, predestined terms, provision from God, and Satan's whispering.

Core Idea
Sunni creed declares Paradise and Hell created, death by appointed term, provision from God, and Satan's influence on humans.

Imam al-Ash'ari continues with essential creedal articles concerning the afterlife and divine decree. These beliefs counter the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya who denied the createdness of Paradise and Hell and Satan's whispering, while affirming that God controls all provision and terms.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage
Read the Arabic and translation. Notice the Quranic evidence provided for Satan's whispering.
2
Identify each article
List: prayer over all Muslims (righteous and sinful), Paradise and Hell created, death by appointed term, provision from God (halal and haram), Satan's whispering.
The last article includes a Quranic proof text.
3
Understand the polemic
The text explicitly opposes Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya on Satan's whispering. Consider why this was controversial.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some think Satan has no real influence over humans or that Paradise and Hell are not yet created.
Correction: Sunni creed affirms that Satan actively whispers and confounds, and Paradise and Hell are already created.

Checkpoint
According to this passage, what is the Sunni stance on the funeral prayer for sinful people?
A) It is not offered for them.
B) It is offered only for the righteous.
C) It is offered for both righteous and sinful.
D) It is optional.

Key Takeaway
Sunni creed includes prayer for all Muslims, created Paradise and Hell, predestined death, divine provision, and Satan's real influence as per Quran.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
When you receive a provision or face a difficulty, do you truly believe it is from God, or do you attribute it solely to your own efforts? How would your trust in God change if you internalized that even the unlawful provision is decreed by Him?
?
Have you ever blamed your sins on Satan's whispering while ignoring your own choice? This lesson affirms Satan's influence, but also holds you accountable. Where in your life do you need to take responsibility rather than blame the shaytan?
?
If you really believed that Paradise and Hell are created and present now, would your urgency to seek Paradise and avoid Hell be stronger? What is one concrete change you can make today because of this truth?

Creed: Saints' Miracles, Children of Polytheists, God's Knowledge, and Separation
Lists final creedal articles: miracles of saints, fate of polytheists' children, God's knowledge, obedience, and separation from innovators.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the Sunni positions on miracles of saints, the test for polytheists' children in the afterlife, God's comprehensive knowledge, and the duty to avoid heretical misguidance.

Core Idea
Sunni creed affirms saintly miracles, a specific test for polytheists' children, God's exhaustive knowledge, and avoidance of innovators.

Imam al-Ash'ari concludes his creed with articles that clarify further aspects of divine power and human responsibility. These include the permissibility of saintly miracles, the fate of polytheists' children as a test, God's all-encompassing knowledge, and the final command to distance from those who call to innovation.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage
Read the Arabic and translation. Note the variety of topics: miracles, children, God's knowledge, obedience, separation.
2
Examine each article
Identify: 1) Saints may be given signs (miracles), 2) Children of polytheists will be tested with fire, 3) God's knowledge includes all possibilities, 4) Obedience to imams, 5) Separation from innovators.
3
Reflect on the conclusion
The passage ends by emphasizing separation from people of desires and innovations, marking the boundary of Sunni identity.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
وأن المسح على الخفين سنة في الحضر والسفر ، خلافاً لمن أنكر ذلك ، ونرى الدعاء لأئمة المسلمين بالصلاح والإقرار بإمامتهم وتضليل من رأى الخروج عليهم إذا ظهر منهم ترك الاستقامة . وندين بإنكار الخروج بالسيف وترك القتال في الفتنة ، ونقر بخروج الدجال كما جاءت به الرواية عن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم. ونؤمن بعذاب القبر ونكير ومنكر ومسائلتهما المدفونين بقبورهم ونصدق بحديث المعراج . ونصحح كثيراً من الرؤيا في المنام ونقر أن لذلك تفسيراً . ونرى الصدقة عن موتى المسلمين والدعاء لهم : ونؤمن أن الله ينفعهم بذلك ، ونصدق بأن في الدنيا سحرة وسحراً ، وأن السحر كائن موجود في الدنيا . وندين بالصلاة على من مات من أهل القبلة برهم وفاجرهم وتوراثهم . ونقر أن الجنة والنار مخلوقتان ، وأن من مات وقتل فبأجله مات وقتل وأن الأرزاق من قبل الله يرزقها عباده حلالاً وحراماً ، وأن الشيطان يوسوس للإنسان ويسلكه ويتخبطه خلافاً لقول المعتزلة والجهمية ، كما قال الله عز وجل : ( الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبوا لَا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَقُومُ الَّذِى يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ مِنَ المس ) وكما قال : ( مِن شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ الْخَنَّاسِ * الَّذِي يُوَسْوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ الناس مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ . ونقول : إن الصالحين يجوز أن يخصهم الله عز وجل بآيات يظهرها عليهم وقولنا في أطفال المشركين : إن الله يؤجج لهم في الآخرة ناراً ثم يقول لهم اقتحموها ، كما جاءت بذلك الرواية . وندين الله عز وجل بأنه يعلم ما العباد عاملون وإلى ما هم صائرون ، وما كان وما يكون وما لا يكون أن لو كان كيف كان يكون وبطاعة الأئمة المسلمين، ونرى مفارقة كل داعية إلى بدعته ومجانبة أهل الهوى . انتهى بحروفه
And that wiping over the leather socks (al-mash 'ala al-khuffayn) is a sunna in residence and travel, contrary to those who deny that. And we hold that supplication for the Muslim imams for righteousness and acknowledgment of their leadership, and misguidance of those who see rebellion against them when they manifest abandonment of uprightness. And we profess rejection of rebellion with the sword and abandonment of fighting in turmoil (al-fitna), and we affirm the emergence of the Dajjal as the narration from the Messenger of God (peace be upon him) has come. And we believe in the punishment of the grave, and Nakir and Munkar, and their questioning of the buried in their graves, and we affirm the narration of the Night Journey (al-mi'raj). And we consider many dreams in sleep as true and affirm that they have an interpretation. And we hold that charity for the deceased Muslims and supplication for them—and we believe that God benefits them by that—and we affirm that in the world there are sorcerers and sorcery, and that sorcery exists in the world. And we profess prayer over whoever dies from the people of the qibla, their righteous and their sinful, and their inheritance [among each other]. And we affirm that Paradise and Hell are created, and that whoever dies or is killed dies and is killed by his appointed term, and that provisions are from God, He provides them to His servants, lawful and unlawful, and that Satan whispers to man and leads him and confounds him, contrary to the claim of the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya, as God Almighty said: (Those who consume interest will not rise except as one rises whom Satan has confounded by touch) and as He said: (From the evil of the whisperer who withdraws, who whispers in the breasts of people, from among the jinn and people). And we say: It is permissible for God Almighty to distinguish the righteous with signs that He manifests upon them. And our statement concerning the children of polytheists: that God will kindle for them a fire in the Hereafter, then say to them: Leap into it, as the narration has come. And we profess to God Almighty that He knows what the servants are doing and to what they are progressing, and what was and what will be and what would not be if it were to be, how it would be. And [we profess] obedience to the Muslim imams. And we hold that we should separate from every caller to his innovation and shun the people of desires. It is concluded verbatim.
Source pages p. 26

Commentary
This passage is a comprehensive list of creedal articles that form the core of Ahl al-Sunnah according to Imam al-Ash'ari. It begins by affirming the sunna of wiping over leather socks during travel and residence, countering those who reject it. It then addresses political obedience: supplication for Muslim rulers, acknowledgment of their legitimacy, and condemnation of rebellion even if they deviate. Rebellion with the sword is rejected, and fighting during fitna is abandoned. The passage then moves to eschatological beliefs: the emergence of the Dajjal, punishment of the grave, the angels Nakir and Munkar questioning the dead, and the truth of the Night Journey (Isra and Mi'raj). Dreams are affirmed as having true interpretations. Charity for deceased Muslims is beneficial, and sorcery exists as a reality in the world. Prayer is offered over all Muslims of the qibla regardless of righteousness, and inheritance is recognized. Paradise and Hell are already created. Death occurs by appointed term, and provision is from God, both lawful and unlawful. Satan's whispering and influence on humans is affirmed, supported by Quranic verses against the Mu'tazila and Jahmiyya who deny this. The possibility of miracles for saints is affirmed. The fate of polytheists' children is specified as a test with fire in the afterlife. God's knowledge encompasses all—past, present, future, and even hypotheticals. Obedience to Muslim imams is required. Finally, separation from innovators and people of desires is mandated. The passage ends with 'concluded verbatim' marking the end of the creed.

Key Terms
آيات الصالحين
أطفال المشركين
البدعة
أهل الهوى

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some think polytheists' children are automatically condemned or that saints' miracles are not possible.
Correction: Sunni creed holds that polytheists' children will be tested, and saints may be given miracles by God.

Checkpoint
What does the text say about the children of polytheists in the afterlife?
A) They will enter Paradise.
B) They will enter Hell forever.
C) They will be tested with a fire.
D) They will not be resurrected.

Key Takeaway
The creed concludes by affirming saintly miracles, a test for polytheists' children, God's all-encompassing knowledge, obedience to rulers, and the duty to separate from innovators.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
You claim to believe that God knows every hypothetical scenario in your life, including your unacted thoughts. Does that knowledge change how you view your past regrets and hidden intentions? Will you today confess to Him a secret thought you've never spoken, knowing He already knows it?
?
The creed commands you to separate from every caller to innovation. Are you currently connected—online or in person—to anyone who openly promotes a new practice in religion that contradicts the Sunnah? What concrete step will you take this week to distance yourself from that influence?
?
You affirm that God grants miracles to saints. Have you ever dismissed a reported miracle as superstition, thereby belittling Allah's power to honor His servants? Ask yourself: if a true miracle occurred before you today, would your heart be ready to accept it as a sign from God?
