We Affirm All That Comes from Allah
The author affirms comprehensive acceptance of divine revelation.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that the Islamic creed begins with accepting all that is authentically revealed, without rejection.

Core Idea
The creed starts with acceptance, not rejection.

Imam Ash'ari begins the summary of the creed by establishing the foundational attitude of a believer: we affirm everything that comes from Allah and His Messenger. This sets the tone for the entire article of faith.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage carefully
Read the Arabic text and its translation to grasp the comprehensive nature of the affirmation.
Notice the phrase 'لا نرد من ذلك شيئاً' – we reject none of that.
2
Identify the items affirmed
List all the things the passage says we affirm: Allah, angels, books, messengers, what came from Allah, and what trustworthy narrators transmitted.
3
Understand the principle
Reflect on why the author emphasizes 'we reject none of that'. This establishes that selective belief is not sufficient.
This principle guards against rejecting any part of revelation.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
٢٥ - وجملة قولنا : إِنَّا نُقِرُّ بالله وملائكته وكتبه ورسله، وبما جاء من عند الله ، وما رواه الثقات عن رسول الله ، لا نرد من ذلك شيئاً (3) وأن الله عزّ وجل إله واحد لا إله إلا هو فرد صمد، لم يتخذ صاحبة ولا ولدا (1). وأن محمداً عبده ورسوله أرسله بالهدى ودين الحق (2). ٢٦ - وأن الجنة حق والنار حق (3) ، وأن الساعة آتية لا ريب فيها، وأن الله يبعث من في القبور (4)
25 - The summary of our statement: We affirm Allah, His angels, His books, His messengers, and what came from Allah, and what the trustworthy have narrated from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ; we reject none of that. And that Allah, Mighty and Majestic, is one God, there is no god but Him, unique, eternal, He has not taken a companion nor a son. And that Muhammad is His servant and His messenger, He sent him with guidance and the religion of truth. 26 - And that Paradise is true, and Hellfire is true, and that the Hour is coming, no doubt in it, and that Allah will resurrect those in the graves.
Source pages p. 43

Commentary
This passage lists the fundamental articles of faith. The author begins with a general acceptance of all that comes from Allah and is authentically transmitted from the Prophet. Then specifically affirms tawhid (oneness of Allah), rejecting polytheism. Then affirms Muhammad's prophethood. Then affirms the afterlife: Paradise, Hell, the Hour, and resurrection. These are the core beliefs that all Muslims must hold.

Key Terms
نقر بالله
الثقات

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: One can accept some parts of revelation and reject others.
Correction: The passage explicitly says 'we reject none of that', making it obligatory to accept all authentic revelation.

Checkpoint
Which of the following is NOT included in the things we affirm according to this passage?
A) Allah
B) angels
C) the Quran
D) predestination

Key Takeaway
The believer's stance is to accept all divine revelation without exception.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
Is there any part of Allah's revelation—whether from the Qur'an or authentic hadith—that you have resisted or ignored? What would it mean for your faith to fully embrace 'we reject none of that'?
?
Do you treat the Sunnah with the same acceptance as the Qur'an? The passage includes 'what the trustworthy narrated'—what would change if you gave equal weight to authentic hadith in your daily life?
?
This passage begins with acceptance. Is your faith primarily defined by what you affirm or what you reject? How could you shift to a posture of full affirmation, and what is the first step you will take?

Affirming Tawhid and Prophethood
The author articulates tawhid and prophethood as essential beliefs.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the core beliefs of tawhid (Allah's oneness) and prophethood as summarized in the creed.

Core Idea
Allah is one, unique, eternal; Muhammad is His servant and messenger.

After the general affirmation, the author specifies two central doctrines: the oneness of Allah and the prophethood of Muhammad. These are the pillars of Islamic faith.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage on tawhid
Read the sentence about Allah: 'إله واحد لا إله إلا هو فرد صمد...' and its translation.
Note the attributes: 'fard' (unique), 'samad' (eternal).
2
Analyze the tawhid statement
Understand that Allah is one, without partner or child, and that He is self-sufficient.
3
Read the prophethood statement
Read about Muhammad: 'عبده ورسوله' and his mission with guidance and truth.
He is both servant and messenger, emphasizing his humanity.
4
Connect the two
See that tawhid and prophethood are inseparable – the message confirms the oneness.

Allah's Existence
intermediate
6 min
Quranic Proof That God Wills Fighting
Al-Ash'ari uses Quranic verses to argue that God wills the fighting that occurs.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand how Al-Ash'ari uses Quranic verses and conditional reasoning to prove that God wills all fighting that occurs.

Core Idea
Fighting occurs because God wills it; otherwise it would not happen.

This lesson presents a concise Quranic argument from Imam Al-Ash'ari. He cites two verses to show that the actual occurrence of fighting proves that God willed it. The logic is simple: if God had willed otherwise, fighting would not have taken place. This refutes the claim that evil events happen without God's will.

Guided Path
1
Read the Arabic passage and translation
Read the Arabic text once to get its rhythm, then read the literal translation. Notice the conditional structures.
The passage uses 'law' (لو) conditionals.
2
Understand the main argument from the first verse
The first verse says: 'If Allah had willed, they would not have fought.' This implies a conditional necessity. Since fighting does occur, we can deduce that God did not will to prevent it—i.e., He willed it.
This is a straightforward logical inference (modus tollens).
3
Examine the confirmation from the second verse
The second verse, 'But Allah does what He wills,' reinforces that God's will is effective regarding fighting. Whatever happens, He wills it.
This verse is general, but applied specifically to fighting.
4
See the parallel with the disbelievers verse
The third verse about disbelievers: if they were sent back, they would return to disbelief. Their actual state (not sent back) shows that God did not will to send them back. Similarly, the actuality of fighting shows God willed it.
This strengthens the argument by analogy.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
ويقال لهم : قال الله عز وجل : ﴿ وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللهُ مَا أَقْتَتَلُوا فأخبر أنه لو شاء أن لا يقتتلوا ما اقتتلوا، قال: ﴿وَلَكِنَّ اللَّهَ يَفْعَلُ مَا يُرِيدُ ﴾ [البقرة: ٢٥٣] من القتال، فإذا وقع القتال فقد شاءه، كما أنه قال: ﴿وَلَوْ رُدُّوا لَعَادُوا لِمَا نُهُوا عَنْهُ ﴾ [الأنعام : ۲۸] فقد أوجب أن الرد لو كان إلى الدنيا لعادوا إلى الكفر، وأنهم إذا لم يردّهم إلى الدنيا لم يعودوا ، فكذلك لو شاء الله أن لا يقتتلوا لما اقتتلوا، وإذا اقتتلوا فقد شاء أن يقتتلوا .
And it is said to them: Allah, mighty and majestic, said: 'And if Allah had willed, they would not have fought' — so He informed that if He had willed that they not fight, they would not have fought. He said: 'But Allah does what He wills' (al-Baqarah 2:253) regarding fighting. So when fighting occurs, He has willed it. Similarly, He said: 'And if they were sent back, they would return to what they were forbidden from' (al-An'am 6:28). So He established that if they were sent back to the world, they would return to disbelief, and if He does not send them back to the world, they will not return. Likewise, if Allah had willed that they not fight, they would not have fought; and if they fought, then He has willed that they fight.
Source pages p. 129

Commentary
This argument (hujja) uses explicit Quranic texts. The verse 'if Allah had willed, they would not have fought' implies conditional necessity: if God had willed non-fighting, it would have happened. But since fighting does occur, we can infer that God willed it. Another verse 'But Allah does what He wills' affirms that God's will is effective. Al-Ash'ari then parallels with the verse about disbelievers: if they were sent back, they would return to disbelief. This conditional shows that their actual state is not being sent back. So analogously, the actual occurrence of fighting proves that God willed it. The argument refutes those who claim that evil occurs without God's will.

Key Terms
شاء (sha'a)
المشيئة (al-mashi'a)
القتال (al-qital)

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: That God does not will evil events like fighting; they occur without His will.
Correction: The verses show that if God did not will fighting, it would not occur. Since fighting does occur, it must be that God willed it.

Checkpoint
According to the verse 'If Allah had willed, they would not have fought,' what does the occurrence of fighting indicate?
A) That God has no control over fighting
B) That God willed the fighting
C) That fighting is against God's will
D) That God's will is conditional

Key Takeaway
The occurrence of fighting proves that God willed it, because if He had willed otherwise it would not have occurred.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
When you witness conflict or injustice in the world, do you honestly accept that it occurs by God's will, or do you find yourself questioning His wisdom? What would change in your heart if you truly believed that every event—even the most painful—is willed by Allah?
?
Have you ever used the concept of 'not God's will' to justify your own lack of effort or responsibility? How does this lesson challenge you to see that all that happens is under His will, and what does that mean for how you respond to trials?

Prophethood
beginner
5 min
Testimony of Prophethood and Authority of Quran and Sunnah
Ash'ari testifies to Muhammad's prophethood, extols the Quran, and emphasizes following the Sunnah.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the Sunni testimony of Muhammad's prophethood and the fundamental authority of the Quran and Sunnah in Islam.

Core Idea
Belief in the Prophet, Quran, and Sunnah is the foundation of guidance.

Imam al-Ash'ari presents a profound testimony of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) and the divine scriptures. This passage not only affirms his prophethood but also elevates the Quran as the unassailable word of God and the Sunnah as its necessary complement. As you read, notice how the author weaves together praise of the Prophet, description of the Quran's perfection, and Quranic commands to follow the Sunnah.

Guided Path
1
Read the Arabic passage slowly
Read the Arabic text once without stopping. Pay attention to the rhythm and the shift from praising the Prophet to describing the Quran and then quoting verses.
2
Identify the three segments
The passage has three main parts: (1) testimony of prophethood, (2) praise of the Quran, (3) command to follow the Sunnah with Quranic evidence.
3
Reflect on the Quranic verses
Notice the four verses quoted about obedience to the Messenger. They show that the Sunnah is an authoritative source of Islamic law.
4
Consider the implications
Reflect on the statement that the Quran contains knowledge of the first and the last, and that holding fast to it saves one. This establishes the Quran as the primary source of guidance.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
ونشهد أن محمداً عبده ونبيه ورسوله إلى خلقه، وأمينه على وحيه، أرسله إلى خلقه بالنور الساطع والسراج اللامع والحجج الظاهرة والبراهين والآيات الباهرة والأعاجيب القاهرة، فبلغ عن الله رسالاته، ونصح له في بريته، وجاهد في الله حق جهاده، وقاتل أهل العناد، حتى تمت كلمة الله عز وجل، وظهر أمره، وانقاد الناس للحق أجمعين حتى أتاه اليقين لا وانياً ولا مقصراً، فصلوات الله عليه من قائد إلى الهدى، مبين عن ضلالة وعمي، وعلى آل بيته الطيبين، وعلى أصحابه المنتخبين، وعلى أزواجه الطاهرات أمهات المؤمنين. عرفنا الله به الشرائع والأحكام والحلال والحرام، وبين لنا به شريعة الإسلام، حتى انجلت عنا طخياء الظلام، وانحسرت به عنا الشبهات، وانكشفت به عنا الغيابات، وظهرت لنا به البينات. وجاءنا بكتاب عزيز لا يأتيه الباطل من بين يديه ولا من خلفه تنزيل من حكيم حميد، جمع فيه علم الأولين والآخرين، وأكمل به الفرائض والدين، فهو صراط الله المستقيم، وحبله المتين، من تمسك به نجا، ومن خالفه ضل وغوى، وفي الجهل تردى، وحث الله في كتابه على التمسك بسنة رسوله، فقال عز وجل: وما آتاكم الرسول فخذوه وما نهاكم عنه فانتهوا، وقال عز وجل: فليحذر الذين يخالفون عن أمره أن تصيبهم فتنة أو يصيبهم عذاب أليم، وقال: ولو ردوه إلى الرسول وإلى أولي الأمر منهم لعلمه الذين يستنبطونه منهم، وقال: وما اختلفتم فيه من شيء فحكمه إلى الله، يقول: إلى كتاب الله وسنة نبيه.
And we bear witness that Muhammad is His servant, His Prophet, and His Messenger to His creation, and His trustee over His revelation. He sent him to His creation with the shining light, the luminous lamp, the clear proofs, the brilliant evidences and miracles, and the overwhelming wonders. He conveyed Allah's messages, advised His creatures, strove in Allah with true striving, and fought the people of obstinacy until the word of Allah, Mighty and Majestic, was fulfilled, and His command prevailed, and the people submitted to the truth entirely until certainty came to him, neither weary nor remiss. So the blessings of Allah be upon him, a guide to guidance, an elucidator of error and blindness, and upon his pure family, his chosen companions, and his pure wives, the mothers of the believers. Through him, Allah made known to us the laws, rulings, halal and haram, and through him He explained to us the Shariah of Islam, until the dense darkness cleared from us, doubts dissipated, hidden things were uncovered, and clear signs appeared to us. And he brought us a mighty Book: 'Falsehood cannot approach it from before or behind; a revelation from the Wise, the Praiseworthy.' In it is gathered the knowledge of the first and the last, and through it He completed the obligations and the religion. It is the straight path of Allah, His firm rope. Whoever holds fast to it is saved, whoever opposes it is astray and misguided and plummets into ignorance. And Allah urged in His Book adherence to the Sunnah of His Messenger, saying: 'And whatever the Messenger gives you, take it; and whatever he forbids you, abstain from it.' And He said: 'So let those who oppose his command beware lest a trial befall them or a painful punishment.' And He said: 'And if they had referred it to the Messenger and to those in authority among them, those who derive it from them would know it.' And He said: 'And whatever you differ about, its ruling belongs to Allah,' meaning to the Book of Allah and the Sunnah of His Prophet.
Source pages p. 34

Commentary
This passage is the testimony of prophethood and an extended eulogy of the Prophet Muhammad, followed by a description of the Quran and the necessity of adhering to the Sunnah. The author begins by asserting Muhammad's servitude, prophethood, and messengership. He lists the miraculous signs and wonders given to him. He emphasizes that the Prophet fulfilled his mission completely, fought against obstinacy, and brought the truth until his death. Then he describes how through the Prophet, Allah made the religion clear, dispelling darkness and doubts. He extols the Quran as a divine book beyond falsehood, containing all knowledge, and as the straight path and firm rope of Allah. He then cites several Quranic verses commanding obedience to the Prophet and referring disputes to Allah and His Messenger, underscoring the authority of the Sunnah. This passage establishes the foundational Sunni belief in the complete authority of the Quran and Sunnah.

Key Terms
نبوة
رسالة
القرآن
السنة

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some think mentioning the Prophet's family and companions implies a sectarian bias.
Correction: The passage honors them as a general Sunni practice, not as an exclusive allegiance.

Checkpoint
According to the passage, what is the status of the Quran?
A) It is only for scholars.
B) It will be abrogated.
C) No falsehood can approach it.
D) It is difficult to understand.

Key Takeaway
The Sunni creed firmly holds the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) as the final messenger, the Quran as the untainted word of God, and the Sunnah as its authoritative interpretation.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
The passage says that through the Prophet, Allah made known the laws, halal and haram. Is there any practice in your daily life where you follow cultural customs or personal desires instead of what the Prophet taught? What is one specific change you can make this week to align with his guidance?
?
The Quran is described as the firm rope of Allah; whoever holds fast to it is saved. When was the last time you truly held fast to the Quran beyond recitation—by seeking its guidance for a decision, a doubt, or a trial? What is one verse you will commit to acting upon starting today?
?
The passage cites multiple Quranic commands to obey the Messenger. Are there any sunnah acts (e.g., in prayer, character, or daily habits) that you have been neglecting or treating as optional? What is one sunnah you will revive this week as an act of obedience?

Prophethood
beginner
5 min
The Prophet's Mission and Role
Al-Ash'ari praises the Prophet's mission and his role in clarifying the Shari'ah.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand how Imam al-Ash'ari describes the Prophet Muhammad's mission as a guiding light and the completion of God's command.

Core Idea
The Prophet was sent with clear proofs to guide humanity and fulfill God's word.

Imam al-Ash'ari begins his exhortation by praising the Prophet Muhammad's role as the final messenger. He emphasizes that the Prophet was sent with overwhelming signs and that he fulfilled his duty perfectly, thereby completing God's command. This passage establishes the authority of the Prophet as the guide for all.

Guided Path
1
Read the passage on the Prophet's mission
Read the Arabic passage describing the Prophet's mission carefully. Then read the translation. Note the vivid imagery used.
Focus on the phrases 'radiant light', 'luminous lamp', and 'manifest proofs'.
2
Identify the purpose of the Prophet's mission
Notice that the Prophet delivered God's messages, advised creation, strove and fought until God's word was fulfilled.
3
Reflect on the blessing and the result
The passage ends with blessings and then notes that through the Prophet, God made known laws and dispelled darkness. This shows the Prophet's comprehensive guidance.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
على وحيه، أرسله إلى خلقه بالنور الساطع، والسراج اللامع، والحجج الظاهرة، والبراهين والآيات الباهرة، والأعاجيب القاهرة، فبلغ عن الله رسالاته، ونصح له في بريَّاته، وجاهد في الله حق الجهاد، وقاتل أهل العناد، حتى تمت كلمة الله عَزَّ وجل، وظَهَرَ أَمْرُه، وانقاد الناس للحق أجمعين حتى أتاه اليقين لا وانياً ولا مقصراً فصلوات الله عليه من قائد إلى الهدى، مبين عن ضلالة وعمي وعلى آل بيته الطيبين، وعلى أصحابه المنتخبين، وعلى أزواجه الطاهرات أمهات المؤمنين . عرفنا الله به الشرائع والأحكام والحلال والحرام، وبين لنا به شريعة الإسلام ، حتى انجلت عنا طخياء الظلام، وانحسرت به عنا الشبهات، وانكشفت به عنا الغيابات وظهرت لنا به البينات . وجاءنا بكتاب عزيز ( لَا يَأْتِيهِ الْبَطِلُ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَلَا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ تَنزِيلٌ مِنْ حَكِيمٍ حميد [فصلت: ٤٢] جمع فيه علم الأولين والآخرين، وأكمل به الفرائض والدين، فهو صراط الله المستقيم، وحبله المتين، من تمسك به نجا، ومن خالفه ضل وغوى، وفي الجهل تَرَدَّى ، وحث الله في كتابه على التمسك بسنة رسوله ، فقال عز وجل: ﴿وَمَا آتَاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَمَا نَهَاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانتَهُوا ) [الحشر: ۷] وقال عز وجل: فَلْيَحْذَرِ الَّذِينَ يُخَالِفُونَ عَنْ أَمْرِهِ أَن تُصِيبَهُمْ فِتْنَةٌ أَوْ يُصِيبَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ [النور: ٦٣]. وقال: ﴿وَلَوْ رَدُّوهُ إِلَى الرَّسُولِ وَإِلَى أُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْهُمْ لَعَلِمَهُ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَنْبِطُونَهُ مِنْهُمْ ﴾ [النساء:۸۳] . وقال : ( وَمَا اخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِيهِ مِن شَيْءٍ فَحُكْمُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ ﴾ [الشورى: ١٠] ، يقول : إلى كتاب الله وسنة نبيه

وقال: ﴿ وَمَا يَنطِقُ عَنِ الْهَوَى * إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا وَحْيٌ يُوحَى ) [ النجم : ٣-٤] ، وقال : ( قُل مَا يَكُونُ لِي أَنْ أُبَدِّلَهُ مِن تِلْقَاءِ نَفْسِي إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا مَا يُوحَى إِلَيَّ ) [يونس : ١٥] . وقال : إِنَّمَا كَانَ قَوْلَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذَا دُعُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَن يَقُولُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ) [ النور : ٥١]، فأمرهم أن يسمعوا قوله، ويطيعوا أمره، ويحذروا مخالفته. وقال : أَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ ﴾ [النساء : ٥٩] ، فأمرهم بطاعةِ رسولِهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم كما أمرهم بطاعته، ودعاهم إلى التمسك بسنة نبيه ، كما أمرهم بالعمل بكتابه.

٥ - فَنَبَذَ كثير ممن غلبت عليهم شقوتهم ، واستحوذ عليهم الشيطان سُنَن نبي الله وراء ظهورهم ومالوا إلى أسلاف لهم قلدوهم بدينهم، ودانوا بديانتهم، وأبطلوا سُنَنَ رسول الله ، ورفضوها وأنكروها وجحدوها، افتراء منهم على الله، قَدْ ضَلُّوا وَمَا كَانُوا مُهْتَدِينَ ) [الأنعام: ١٤٠]. أوصيكم عباد الله بتقوى الله ، وأحذركم الدنيا ، فإنها حلوة خضرَةٌ، تغرُّ أَهْلَها ، وَتَخْدَعُ سكانها ، قال الله تعالى : ( وَاضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا كَمَاء أَنزَلْنَاهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فَاخْتَلَطَ بِهِ نَبَاتُ الْأَرْضِ فَأَصْبَحَ هَشِيمًا تَذْرُوهُ الرِّيحُ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُقْتَدِرًا ﴾ [الكهف : ٤٥]. مَن كَانَ فِيهَا فِي حَبْرَةٍ أَعْقَبَتْهُ بَعْدَهَا عِبْرَةً، وَمَنْ أَعْطَتْهُ مِن سَرَّائِهَا بَطْنًا أَعْقَبَتْهُ مِن ضَرَّائِهَا ظَهْرًا، غَرَّارَةٌ، غُرُورٌ مَا فِيهَا، فَانِيَةٌ، فَانٍ مَا عَلَيْهَا، كَمَا حَكَمَ عَلَيْهَا رَبُّهَا بِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى، إِذْ يَقُولُ :
In His revelation, He sent him to His creation with the radiant light, the luminous lamp, the manifest proofs, the brilliant evidences and signs, and the overwhelming wonders. He delivered to them the messages of God, and he advised His creation, and he strove in God with true striving, and fought against the people of obstinacy until the word of God, mighty and sublime, was fulfilled, and His command became manifest, and the people submitted to the truth entirely until certainty came to him, neither faltering nor falling short. So may the blessings of God be upon him, a guide to guidance, a clarifier against error and blindness, and upon his pure family, and upon his chosen companions, and upon his pure wives, the mothers of the believers. Through him, God made known to us the laws (shara'i'), the rulings (ahkam), the lawful and the unlawful, and through him He explained to us the legislation of Islam, until the dense darkness was cleared from us, and doubts were removed from us through him, and obscurities were unveiled from us, and clear proofs became apparent to us through him. And he brought us a mighty Book: 'Falsehood cannot come to it from before it or behind it; a revelation from the Wise, the Praiseworthy' (Fussilat 42). In it is gathered the knowledge of the first and the last, and through it He perfected the obligations and the religion. It is the straight path of God and His strong rope. Whoever holds fast to it is saved, and whoever opposes it goes astray and errs, and falls into ignorance. And God urged in His Book adherence to the Sunnah of His Messenger, for He, may He be exalted, said: 'And whatever the Messenger gives you, take it, and whatever he forbids you, refrain from it' (al-Hashr 7). And He, may He be exalted, said: 'Let those who oppose his command beware lest a trial befall them or a painful punishment befall them' (al-Nur 63). And He said: 'If they had referred it to the Messenger and to those in authority among them, those who deduce it from them would know it' (al-Nisa' 83). And He said: 'And whatever you differ over, its judgment belongs to God' (al-Shura 10). He said: 'To the Book of God and the Sunnah of His Prophet.'

And He said: 'Nor does he speak from desire. It is only a revelation revealed' (al-Najm 3-4). And He said: 'Say: It is not for me to change it of my own accord. I only follow what is revealed to me' (Yunus 15). And He said: 'The only saying of the believers when they are called to God and His Messenger to judge between them is that they say: We hear and we obey' (al-Nur 51). So He commanded them to hear His words and obey His command and beware of opposing Him. And He said: 'Obey God and obey the Messenger' (al-Nisa' 59). So He commanded them to obey His Messenger, peace be upon him, just as He commanded them to obey Him, and He called them to adhere to the Sunnah of His Prophet, just as He commanded them to act upon His Book.

5. So many of those whose wretchedness prevailed over them, and whom Satan gained mastery over, cast the Sunnahs of the Prophet of God behind their backs, and inclined to certain predecessors whom they blindly followed in their religion, and they adopted their religion as their own, and they nullified the Sunnahs of the Messenger of God, and rejected them, denied them, and disavowed them, fabricating lies against God. 'They have gone astray, and they were not guided' (al-An'am 140). I advise you, servants of God, to fear God, and I warn you against this world, for it is sweet and green, it deceives its people and deludes its inhabitants. God Almighty said: 'And strike for them the example of the life of this world: like water which We send down from the sky, and the vegetation of the earth mixes with it, and then it becomes dry chaff that the winds scatter. And God is over all things competent' (al-Kahf 45). Whoever was in it in joy, it brought him after it a lesson; and whoever it gave from its pleasures a bellyful, it brought upon him from its hardships a backful. It is a deceiver; what is in it is delusion; it is perishable; whatever is on it is passing away. As its Lord has decreed concerning it with His saying, when He says: ...
Source pages p. 35

Commentary
This passage is an exhortation by Imam al-Ash'ari on the obligation to follow the Quran and Sunnah and a warning against those who abandon them. The passage begins by praising the Prophet Muhammad's mission: he was sent with clear proofs and overwhelming signs, fulfilled God's command, and guided the people to truth. Al-Ash'ari then highlights the role of the Prophet in explaining the laws and rulings of Islam, dispelling ignorance and doubts. The Quran is described as a mighty book that contains knowledge of all things and is the straight path and strong rope of God; clinging to it ensures salvation, while opposing it leads to error. Al-Ash'ari then cites multiple Quranic verses commanding obedience to the Prophet and adherence to his Sunnah, emphasizing that the Prophet's words are revelation and must be followed. The second part of the passage (section 5) condemns those who abandon the Sunnah and blindly follow their ancestors, accusing them of fabricating lies against God and going astray. Finally, al-Ash'ari delivers a warning about the deceptive nature of this world, citing the Quranic parable of life being like vegetation that withers away, and reminds the audience that worldly joys are temporary and will be followed by hardship. The passage ends abruptly at a verse citation, but the overall message is clear: hold fast to the Quran and Sunnah, beware of blind imitation, and do not be deceived by this world.

Key Terms
رسول
سنة
شريعة

Key Terms from Source Passage

Checkpoint
According to the passage, what did the Prophet bring to humanity?
A) Wealth and power
B) Radiant light and manifest proofs
C) New laws without any connection to previous revelations
D) Political leadership

Key Takeaway
The Prophet Muhammad was sent with clear proofs and divine guidance to lead humanity out of darkness.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
The Prophet brought 'radiant light' and 'luminous lamp' to clear darkness. What area of your life remains in darkness because you have not fully accepted or implemented what the Prophet brought? Will you today allow that light to enter that part of your heart?
?
The Prophet 'strove in God with true striving' and 'fought against the people of obstinacy' until people submitted. Is there any area where you are still obstinate against the truth? What will you do to surrender that area completely to God?
?
Through the Prophet, God made known the lawful and unlawful. Are you following your own desires in any matter where the ruling is already clear? Will you commit today to align your choices with what the Prophet taught, even if it is difficult?

Prophethood

The Quran as the Straight Path
Al-Ash'ari describes the Quran's attributes and warns against opposing it.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand al-Ash'ari's description of the Quran as the straight path and strong rope, and the consequences of holding fast or opposing it.

Core Idea
The Quran is the straight path and strong rope; holding fast saves, opposing leads to error.

After praising the Prophet, al-Ash'ari turns to the Quran, describing its attributes and importance. He emphasizes that it contains knowledge of all things and that it is the straight path and strong rope of God. This passage highlights the central role of the Quran in guiding believers.

Guided Path
1
Read the description of the Quran
Read the Arabic passage describing the Quran as a mighty book. Note the Quranic verse quoted and the attributes mentioned.
The verse is from Surah Fussilat, emphasizing that falsehood cannot approach it.
2
Identify key metaphors
Al-Ash'ari calls the Quran 'the straight path of God' and 'His strong rope.' Think about why these images are powerful.
3
Understand the consequences
The passage states that whoever holds fast to it is saved, and whoever opposes it goes astray and falls into ignorance. This reinforces the necessity of adhering to the Quran.
This directly connects to the overall theme of the lesson.

على وحيه، أرسله إلى خلقه بالنور الساطع، والسراج اللامع، والحجج الظاهرة، والبراهين والآيات الباهرة، والأعاجيب القاهرة، فبلغ عن الله رسالاته، ونصح له في بريَّاته، وجاهد في الله حق الجهاد، وقاتل أهل العناد، حتى تمت كلمة الله عَزَّ وجل، وظَهَرَ أَمْرُه، وانقاد الناس للحق أجمعين حتى أتاه اليقين لا وانياً ولا مقصراً فصلوات الله عليه من قائد إلى الهدى، مبين عن ضلالة وعمي وعلى آل بيته الطيبين، وعلى أصحابه المنتخبين، وعلى أزواجه الطاهرات أمهات المؤمنين . عرفنا الله به الشرائع والأحكام والحلال والحرام، وبين لنا به شريعة الإسلام ، حتى انجلت عنا طخياء الظلام، وانحسرت به عنا الشبهات، وانكشفت به عنا الغيابات وظهرت لنا به البينات . وجاءنا بكتاب عزيز ( لَا يَأْتِيهِ الْبَطِلُ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَلَا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ تَنزِيلٌ مِنْ حَكِيمٍ حميد [فصلت: ٤٢] جمع فيه علم الأولين والآخرين، وأكمل به الفرائض والدين، فهو صراط الله المستقيم، وحبله المتين، من تمسك به نجا، ومن خالفه ضل وغوى، وفي الجهل تَرَدَّى ، وحث الله في كتابه على التمسك بسنة رسوله ، فقال عز وجل: ﴿وَمَا آتَاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَمَا نَهَاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانتَهُوا ) [الحشر: ۷] وقال عز وجل: فَلْيَحْذَرِ الَّذِينَ يُخَالِفُونَ عَنْ أَمْرِهِ أَن تُصِيبَهُمْ فِتْنَةٌ أَوْ يُصِيبَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ [النور: ٦٣]. وقال: ﴿وَلَوْ رَدُّوهُ إِلَى الرَّسُولِ وَإِلَى أُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْهُمْ لَعَلِمَهُ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَنْبِطُونَهُ مِنْهُمْ ﴾ [النساء:۸۳] . وقال : ( وَمَا اخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِيهِ مِن شَيْءٍ فَحُكْمُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ ﴾ [الشورى: ١٠] ، يقول : إلى كتاب الله وسنة نبيه

وقال: ﴿ وَمَا يَنطِقُ عَنِ الْهَوَى * إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا وَحْيٌ يُوحَى ) [ النجم : ٣-٤] ، وقال : ( قُل مَا يَكُونُ لِي أَنْ أُبَدِّلَهُ مِن تِلْقَاءِ نَفْسِي إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا مَا يُوحَى إِلَيَّ ) [يونس : ١٥] . وقال : إِنَّمَا كَانَ قَوْلَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذَا دُعُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَن يَقُولُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ) [ النور : ٥١]، فأمرهم أن يسمعوا قوله، ويطيعوا أمره، ويحذروا مخالفته. وقال : أَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ ﴾ [النساء : ٥٩] ، فأمرهم بطاعةِ رسولِهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم كما أمرهم بطاعته، ودعاهم إلى التمسك بسنة نبيه ، كما أمرهم بالعمل بكتابه.

٥ - فَنَبَذَ كثير ممن غلبت عليهم شقوتهم ، واستحوذ عليهم الشيطان سُنَن نبي الله وراء ظهورهم ومالوا إلى أسلاف لهم قلدوهم بدينهم، ودانوا بديانتهم، وأبطلوا سُنَنَ رسول الله ، ورفضوها وأنكروها وجحدوها، افتراء منهم على الله، قَدْ ضَلُّوا وَمَا كَانُوا مُهْتَدِينَ ) [الأنعام: ١٤٠]. أوصيكم عباد الله بتقوى الله ، وأحذركم الدنيا ، فإنها حلوة خضرَةٌ، تغرُّ أَهْلَها ، وَتَخْدَعُ سكانها ، قال الله تعالى : ( وَاضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا كَمَاء أَنزَلْنَاهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فَاخْتَلَطَ بِهِ نَبَاتُ الْأَرْضِ فَأَصْبَحَ هَشِيمًا تَذْرُوهُ الرِّيحُ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُقْتَدِرًا ﴾ [الكهف : ٤٥]. مَن كَانَ فِيهَا فِي حَبْرَةٍ أَعْقَبَتْهُ بَعْدَهَا عِبْرَةً، وَمَنْ أَعْطَتْهُ مِن سَرَّائِهَا بَطْنًا أَعْقَبَتْهُ مِن ضَرَّائِهَا ظَهْرًا، غَرَّارَةٌ، غُرُورٌ مَا فِيهَا، فَانِيَةٌ، فَانٍ مَا عَلَيْهَا، كَمَا حَكَمَ عَلَيْهَا رَبُّهَا بِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى، إِذْ يَقُولُ :
In His revelation, He sent him to His creation with the radiant light, the luminous lamp, the manifest proofs, the brilliant evidences and signs, and the overwhelming wonders. He delivered to them the messages of God, and he advised His creation, and he strove in God with true striving, and fought against the people of obstinacy until the word of God, mighty and sublime, was fulfilled, and His command became manifest, and the people submitted to the truth entirely until certainty came to him, neither faltering nor falling short. So may the blessings of God be upon him, a guide to guidance, a clarifier against error and blindness, and upon his pure family, and upon his chosen companions, and upon his pure wives, the mothers of the believers. Through him, God made known to us the laws (shara'i'), the rulings (ahkam), the lawful and the unlawful, and through him He explained to us the legislation of Islam, until the dense darkness was cleared from us, and doubts were removed from us through him, and obscurities were unveiled from us, and clear proofs became apparent to us through him. And he brought us a mighty Book: 'Falsehood cannot come to it from before it or behind it; a revelation from the Wise, the Praiseworthy' (Fussilat 42). In it is gathered the knowledge of the first and the last, and through it He perfected the obligations and the religion. It is the straight path of God and His strong rope. Whoever holds fast to it is saved, and whoever opposes it goes astray and errs, and falls into ignorance. And God urged in His Book adherence to the Sunnah of His Messenger, for He, may He be exalted, said: 'And whatever the Messenger gives you, take it, and whatever he forbids you, refrain from it' (al-Hashr 7). And He, may He be exalted, said: 'Let those who oppose his command beware lest a trial befall them or a painful punishment befall them' (al-Nur 63). And He said: 'If they had referred it to the Messenger and to those in authority among them, those who deduce it from them would know it' (al-Nisa' 83). And He said: 'And whatever you differ over, its judgment belongs to God' (al-Shura 10). He said: 'To the Book of God and the Sunnah of His Prophet.'

And He said: 'Nor does he speak from desire. It is only a revelation revealed' (al-Najm 3-4). And He said: 'Say: It is not for me to change it of my own accord. I only follow what is revealed to me' (Yunus 15). And He said: 'The only saying of the believers when they are called to God and His Messenger to judge between them is that they say: We hear and we obey' (al-Nur 51). So He commanded them to hear His words and obey His command and beware of opposing Him. And He said: 'Obey God and obey the Messenger' (al-Nisa' 59). So He commanded them to obey His Messenger, peace be upon him, just as He commanded them to obey Him, and He called them to adhere to the Sunnah of His Prophet, just as He commanded them to act upon His Book.

5. So many of those whose wretchedness prevailed over them, and whom Satan gained mastery over, cast the Sunnahs of the Prophet of God behind their backs, and inclined to certain predecessors whom they blindly followed in their religion, and they adopted their religion as their own, and they nullified the Sunnahs of the Messenger of God, and rejected them, denied them, and disavowed them, fabricating lies against God. 'They have gone astray, and they were not guided' (al-An'am 140). I advise you, servants of God, to fear God, and I warn you against this world, for it is sweet and green, it deceives its people and deludes its inhabitants. God Almighty said: 'And strike for them the example of the life of this world: like water which We send down from the sky, and the vegetation of the earth mixes with it, and then it becomes dry chaff that the winds scatter. And God is over all things competent' (al-Kahf 45). Whoever was in it in joy, it brought him after it a lesson; and whoever it gave from its pleasures a bellyful, it brought upon him from its hardships a backful. It is a deceiver; what is in it is delusion; it is perishable; whatever is on it is passing away. As its Lord has decreed concerning it with His saying, when He says: ...
Source pages p. 35

Commentary
This passage is an exhortation by Imam al-Ash'ari on the obligation to follow the Quran and Sunnah and a warning against those who abandon them. The passage begins by praising the Prophet Muhammad's mission: he was sent with clear proofs and overwhelming signs, fulfilled God's command, and guided the people to truth. Al-Ash'ari then highlights the role of the Prophet in explaining the laws and rulings of Islam, dispelling ignorance and doubts. The Quran is described as a mighty book that contains knowledge of all things and is the straight path and strong rope of God; clinging to it ensures salvation, while opposing it leads to error. Al-Ash'ari then cites multiple Quranic verses commanding obedience to the Prophet and adherence to his Sunnah, emphasizing that the Prophet's words are revelation and must be followed. The second part of the passage (section 5) condemns those who abandon the Sunnah and blindly follow their ancestors, accusing them of fabricating lies against God and going astray. Finally, al-Ash'ari delivers a warning about the deceptive nature of this world, citing the Quranic parable of life being like vegetation that withers away, and reminds the audience that worldly joys are temporary and will be followed by hardship. The passage ends abruptly at a verse citation, but the overall message is clear: hold fast to the Quran and Sunnah, beware of blind imitation, and do not be deceived by this world.

Key Terms
كتاب
سنة

Key Terms from Source Passage

Checkpoint
What does al-Ash'ari say about the Quran in this passage?
A) It is a book that can be changed by scholars.
B) It is the straight path and strong rope of God.
C) It only contains knowledge of the Arabs.
D) It is difficult to understand and requires intermediaries.

Key Takeaway
The Quran is the perfect, inerrant, and comprehensive guide; salvation lies in holding fast to it.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
Do you truly hold fast to the Quran in every aspect of your life, or do you only turn to it when convenient? What area of your life are you currently opposing its guidance?
?
When you read the Quran, do you approach it as the inerrant straight path from God, or do you subconsciously filter it through your own desires? How will you change your reading habit starting today?
?
If the Quran is the strong rope that saves, why do you often rely on other sources for guidance? What is one step you can take this week to make the Quran your primary reference in a decision you are facing?

Prophethood
beginner
6 min
The Obligation to Follow the Sunnah
Al-Ash'ari cites multiple Quranic verses commanding obedience to the Prophet and adherence to his Sunnah.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand the Quranic evidence al-Ash'ari provides for the obligation to obey the Prophet and adhere to his Sunnah.

Core Idea
The Quran commands obedience to the Prophet as it commands obedience to God.

Al-Ash'ari now cites a series of Quranic verses that explicitly establish the authority of the Sunnah. He shows that obeying the Prophet is part of obeying God and that opposing his command invites punishment. This lesson presents the scriptural foundations for the binding nature of the Sunnah.

Guided Path
1
Read the verses cited
Read through the Arabic passage that lists several Quranic verses. Note each verse and what it commands or warns.
There are multiple verses; take your time to see how each contributes.
2
Identify the common theme
All verses emphasize the necessity of obeying the Prophet, taking what he gives, refraining from what he forbids, and referring disputes to him.
3
Reflect on the warning
The passage includes a warning (Surah al-Nur 63) that those who oppose the Prophet's command should beware of trial or painful punishment.
This highlights the serious consequences of neglecting the Sunnah.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
على وحيه، أرسله إلى خلقه بالنور الساطع، والسراج اللامع، والحجج الظاهرة، والبراهين والآيات الباهرة، والأعاجيب القاهرة، فبلغ عن الله رسالاته، ونصح له في بريَّاته، وجاهد في الله حق الجهاد، وقاتل أهل العناد، حتى تمت كلمة الله عَزَّ وجل، وظَهَرَ أَمْرُه، وانقاد الناس للحق أجمعين حتى أتاه اليقين لا وانياً ولا مقصراً فصلوات الله عليه من قائد إلى الهدى، مبين عن ضلالة وعمي وعلى آل بيته الطيبين، وعلى أصحابه المنتخبين، وعلى أزواجه الطاهرات أمهات المؤمنين . عرفنا الله به الشرائع والأحكام والحلال والحرام، وبين لنا به شريعة الإسلام ، حتى انجلت عنا طخياء الظلام، وانحسرت به عنا الشبهات، وانكشفت به عنا الغيابات وظهرت لنا به البينات . وجاءنا بكتاب عزيز ( لَا يَأْتِيهِ الْبَطِلُ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَلَا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ تَنزِيلٌ مِنْ حَكِيمٍ حميد [فصلت: ٤٢] جمع فيه علم الأولين والآخرين، وأكمل به الفرائض والدين، فهو صراط الله المستقيم، وحبله المتين، من تمسك به نجا، ومن خالفه ضل وغوى، وفي الجهل تَرَدَّى ، وحث الله في كتابه على التمسك بسنة رسوله ، فقال عز وجل: ﴿وَمَا آتَاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَمَا نَهَاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانتَهُوا ) [الحشر: ۷] وقال عز وجل: فَلْيَحْذَرِ الَّذِينَ يُخَالِفُونَ عَنْ أَمْرِهِ أَن تُصِيبَهُمْ فِتْنَةٌ أَوْ يُصِيبَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ [النور: ٦٣]. وقال: ﴿وَلَوْ رَدُّوهُ إِلَى الرَّسُولِ وَإِلَى أُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْهُمْ لَعَلِمَهُ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَنْبِطُونَهُ مِنْهُمْ ﴾ [النساء:۸۳] . وقال : ( وَمَا اخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِيهِ مِن شَيْءٍ فَحُكْمُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ ﴾ [الشورى: ١٠] ، يقول : إلى كتاب الله وسنة نبيه

وقال: ﴿ وَمَا يَنطِقُ عَنِ الْهَوَى * إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا وَحْيٌ يُوحَى ) [ النجم : ٣-٤] ، وقال : ( قُل مَا يَكُونُ لِي أَنْ أُبَدِّلَهُ مِن تِلْقَاءِ نَفْسِي إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا مَا يُوحَى إِلَيَّ ) [يونس : ١٥] . وقال : إِنَّمَا كَانَ قَوْلَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذَا دُعُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَن يَقُولُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ) [ النور : ٥١]، فأمرهم أن يسمعوا قوله، ويطيعوا أمره، ويحذروا مخالفته. وقال : أَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ ﴾ [النساء : ٥٩] ، فأمرهم بطاعةِ رسولِهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم كما أمرهم بطاعته، ودعاهم إلى التمسك بسنة نبيه ، كما أمرهم بالعمل بكتابه.

٥ - فَنَبَذَ كثير ممن غلبت عليهم شقوتهم ، واستحوذ عليهم الشيطان سُنَن نبي الله وراء ظهورهم ومالوا إلى أسلاف لهم قلدوهم بدينهم، ودانوا بديانتهم، وأبطلوا سُنَنَ رسول الله ، ورفضوها وأنكروها وجحدوها، افتراء منهم على الله، قَدْ ضَلُّوا وَمَا كَانُوا مُهْتَدِينَ ) [الأنعام: ١٤٠]. أوصيكم عباد الله بتقوى الله ، وأحذركم الدنيا ، فإنها حلوة خضرَةٌ، تغرُّ أَهْلَها ، وَتَخْدَعُ سكانها ، قال الله تعالى : ( وَاضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا كَمَاء أَنزَلْنَاهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فَاخْتَلَطَ بِهِ نَبَاتُ الْأَرْضِ فَأَصْبَحَ هَشِيمًا تَذْرُوهُ الرِّيحُ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُقْتَدِرًا ﴾ [الكهف : ٤٥]. مَن كَانَ فِيهَا فِي حَبْرَةٍ أَعْقَبَتْهُ بَعْدَهَا عِبْرَةً، وَمَنْ أَعْطَتْهُ مِن سَرَّائِهَا بَطْنًا أَعْقَبَتْهُ مِن ضَرَّائِهَا ظَهْرًا، غَرَّارَةٌ، غُرُورٌ مَا فِيهَا، فَانِيَةٌ، فَانٍ مَا عَلَيْهَا، كَمَا حَكَمَ عَلَيْهَا رَبُّهَا بِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى، إِذْ يَقُولُ :
In His revelation, He sent him to His creation with the radiant light, the luminous lamp, the manifest proofs, the brilliant evidences and signs, and the overwhelming wonders. He delivered to them the messages of God, and he advised His creation, and he strove in God with true striving, and fought against the people of obstinacy until the word of God, mighty and sublime, was fulfilled, and His command became manifest, and the people submitted to the truth entirely until certainty came to him, neither faltering nor falling short. So may the blessings of God be upon him, a guide to guidance, a clarifier against error and blindness, and upon his pure family, and upon his chosen companions, and upon his pure wives, the mothers of the believers. Through him, God made known to us the laws (shara'i'), the rulings (ahkam), the lawful and the unlawful, and through him He explained to us the legislation of Islam, until the dense darkness was cleared from us, and doubts were removed from us through him, and obscurities were unveiled from us, and clear proofs became apparent to us through him. And he brought us a mighty Book: 'Falsehood cannot come to it from before it or behind it; a revelation from the Wise, the Praiseworthy' (Fussilat 42). In it is gathered the knowledge of the first and the last, and through it He perfected the obligations and the religion. It is the straight path of God and His strong rope. Whoever holds fast to it is saved, and whoever opposes it goes astray and errs, and falls into ignorance. And God urged in His Book adherence to the Sunnah of His Messenger, for He, may He be exalted, said: 'And whatever the Messenger gives you, take it, and whatever he forbids you, refrain from it' (al-Hashr 7). And He, may He be exalted, said: 'Let those who oppose his command beware lest a trial befall them or a painful punishment befall them' (al-Nur 63). And He said: 'If they had referred it to the Messenger and to those in authority among them, those who deduce it from them would know it' (al-Nisa' 83). And He said: 'And whatever you differ over, its judgment belongs to God' (al-Shura 10). He said: 'To the Book of God and the Sunnah of His Prophet.'

And He said: 'Nor does he speak from desire. It is only a revelation revealed' (al-Najm 3-4). And He said: 'Say: It is not for me to change it of my own accord. I only follow what is revealed to me' (Yunus 15). And He said: 'The only saying of the believers when they are called to God and His Messenger to judge between them is that they say: We hear and we obey' (al-Nur 51). So He commanded them to hear His words and obey His command and beware of opposing Him. And He said: 'Obey God and obey the Messenger' (al-Nisa' 59). So He commanded them to obey His Messenger, peace be upon him, just as He commanded them to obey Him, and He called them to adhere to the Sunnah of His Prophet, just as He commanded them to act upon His Book.

5. So many of those whose wretchedness prevailed over them, and whom Satan gained mastery over, cast the Sunnahs of the Prophet of God behind their backs, and inclined to certain predecessors whom they blindly followed in their religion, and they adopted their religion as their own, and they nullified the Sunnahs of the Messenger of God, and rejected them, denied them, and disavowed them, fabricating lies against God. 'They have gone astray, and they were not guided' (al-An'am 140). I advise you, servants of God, to fear God, and I warn you against this world, for it is sweet and green, it deceives its people and deludes its inhabitants. God Almighty said: 'And strike for them the example of the life of this world: like water which We send down from the sky, and the vegetation of the earth mixes with it, and then it becomes dry chaff that the winds scatter. And God is over all things competent' (al-Kahf 45). Whoever was in it in joy, it brought him after it a lesson; and whoever it gave from its pleasures a bellyful, it brought upon him from its hardships a backful. It is a deceiver; what is in it is delusion; it is perishable; whatever is on it is passing away. As its Lord has decreed concerning it with His saying, when He says: ...
Source pages p. 35

Commentary
This passage is an exhortation by Imam al-Ash'ari on the obligation to follow the Quran and Sunnah and a warning against those who abandon them. The passage begins by praising the Prophet Muhammad's mission: he was sent with clear proofs and overwhelming signs, fulfilled God's command, and guided the people to truth. Al-Ash'ari then highlights the role of the Prophet in explaining the laws and rulings of Islam, dispelling ignorance and doubts. The Quran is described as a mighty book that contains knowledge of all things and is the straight path and strong rope of God; clinging to it ensures salvation, while opposing it leads to error. Al-Ash'ari then cites multiple Quranic verses commanding obedience to the Prophet and adherence to his Sunnah, emphasizing that the Prophet's words are revelation and must be followed. The second part of the passage (section 5) condemns those who abandon the Sunnah and blindly follow their ancestors, accusing them of fabricating lies against God and going astray. Finally, al-Ash'ari delivers a warning about the deceptive nature of this world, citing the Quranic parable of life being like vegetation that withers away, and reminds the audience that worldly joys are temporary and will be followed by hardship. The passage ends abruptly at a verse citation, but the overall message is clear: hold fast to the Quran and Sunnah, beware of blind imitation, and do not be deceived by this world.

Key Terms
سنة
طاعة
اتباع

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some think the Quran alone is sufficient and the Sunnah is optional.
Correction: Al-Ash'ari shows that the Quran itself commands obedience to the Prophet and adherence to his Sunnah, making it a necessary source of guidance.

Checkpoint
According to the passage, what does the verse 'Let those who oppose his command beware' (al-Nur 63) indicate?
A) It is permissible to disregard some of the Prophet's commands.
B) Opposing the Prophet's command can lead to trial or punishment.
C) The Prophet's command is only advisory.
D) Only major sins are punishable.

Key Takeaway
The Quran repeatedly commands obedience to the Prophet and adherence to his Sunnah, making it an integral part of Islamic guidance.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
When you receive a command from the Sunnah that conflicts with your personal desires, do you immediately respond with 'We hear and we obey' (al-Nur 51), or do you hesitate and rationalize? What does your hesitation reveal about the place of the Sunnah in your heart? This week, commit to acting on one command from the Sunnah that you have been avoiding.
?
You claim to love the Prophet, but do you truly treat his words as binding? Al-Najm 3-4 tells you that he does not speak from desire—so when you ignore a hadith because it is inconvenient, you are dismissing divine revelation. Where in your life have you been selectively following the Sunnah? Repent and resolve to correct that area today.
?
If God equates obedience to the Messenger with obedience to Himself (al-Nisa' 59), then your attitude toward the Sunnah is a direct measure of your obedience to God. What would a 24-hour audit of your actions reveal about your actual submission? Write down three situations today where you will consciously prioritize the Sunnah over your own opinion.

Prophethood
beginner
4 min
Condemnation of Abandoning the Sunnah and Blind Imitation
Al-Ash'ari condemns those who abandon the Sunnah and blindly follow their ancestors.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand al-Ash'ari's strong condemnation of those who abandon the Sunnah and blindly follow their ancestors.

Core Idea
Abandoning the Sunnah for blind imitation of predecessors is misguidance and fabrication against God.

After establishing the authority of the Sunnah, al-Ash'ari now sharply criticizes those who reject it in favor of blind following (taqlid). He describes them as being overcome by wretchedness and succumbing to Satan, and he accuses them of fabricating lies against God. This lesson highlights the dangers of abandoning revealed guidance for human tradition.

Guided Path
1
Read the condemnation passage
Read the Arabic passage that begins with 'So many of those whose wretchedness prevailed...' and its translation.
Note the strong language: 'cast the Sunnahs behind their backs.'
2
Identify the criticism
Al-Ash'ari criticizes blind imitation (taqlid) of predecessors and the rejection of the Sunnah. He calls it a fabrication against God.
3
Reflect on the outcome
The passage ends with the Quranic verse: 'They have gone astray, and they were not guided.' This seals the condemnation.
This is a serious warning about the consequences of abandoning the Sunnah.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
على وحيه، أرسله إلى خلقه بالنور الساطع، والسراج اللامع، والحجج الظاهرة، والبراهين والآيات الباهرة، والأعاجيب القاهرة، فبلغ عن الله رسالاته، ونصح له في بريَّاته، وجاهد في الله حق الجهاد، وقاتل أهل العناد، حتى تمت كلمة الله عَزَّ وجل، وظَهَرَ أَمْرُه، وانقاد الناس للحق أجمعين حتى أتاه اليقين لا وانياً ولا مقصراً فصلوات الله عليه من قائد إلى الهدى، مبين عن ضلالة وعمي وعلى آل بيته الطيبين، وعلى أصحابه المنتخبين، وعلى أزواجه الطاهرات أمهات المؤمنين . عرفنا الله به الشرائع والأحكام والحلال والحرام، وبين لنا به شريعة الإسلام ، حتى انجلت عنا طخياء الظلام، وانحسرت به عنا الشبهات، وانكشفت به عنا الغيابات وظهرت لنا به البينات . وجاءنا بكتاب عزيز ( لَا يَأْتِيهِ الْبَطِلُ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَلَا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ تَنزِيلٌ مِنْ حَكِيمٍ حميد [فصلت: ٤٢] جمع فيه علم الأولين والآخرين، وأكمل به الفرائض والدين، فهو صراط الله المستقيم، وحبله المتين، من تمسك به نجا، ومن خالفه ضل وغوى، وفي الجهل تَرَدَّى ، وحث الله في كتابه على التمسك بسنة رسوله ، فقال عز وجل: ﴿وَمَا آتَاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَمَا نَهَاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانتَهُوا ) [الحشر: ۷] وقال عز وجل: فَلْيَحْذَرِ الَّذِينَ يُخَالِفُونَ عَنْ أَمْرِهِ أَن تُصِيبَهُمْ فِتْنَةٌ أَوْ يُصِيبَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ [النور: ٦٣]. وقال: ﴿وَلَوْ رَدُّوهُ إِلَى الرَّسُولِ وَإِلَى أُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْهُمْ لَعَلِمَهُ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَنْبِطُونَهُ مِنْهُمْ ﴾ [النساء:۸۳] . وقال : ( وَمَا اخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِيهِ مِن شَيْءٍ فَحُكْمُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ ﴾ [الشورى: ١٠] ، يقول : إلى كتاب الله وسنة نبيه

وقال: ﴿ وَمَا يَنطِقُ عَنِ الْهَوَى * إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا وَحْيٌ يُوحَى ) [ النجم : ٣-٤] ، وقال : ( قُل مَا يَكُونُ لِي أَنْ أُبَدِّلَهُ مِن تِلْقَاءِ نَفْسِي إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا مَا يُوحَى إِلَيَّ ) [يونس : ١٥] . وقال : إِنَّمَا كَانَ قَوْلَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذَا دُعُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَن يَقُولُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ) [ النور : ٥١]، فأمرهم أن يسمعوا قوله، ويطيعوا أمره، ويحذروا مخالفته. وقال : أَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ ﴾ [النساء : ٥٩] ، فأمرهم بطاعةِ رسولِهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم كما أمرهم بطاعته، ودعاهم إلى التمسك بسنة نبيه ، كما أمرهم بالعمل بكتابه.

٥ - فَنَبَذَ كثير ممن غلبت عليهم شقوتهم ، واستحوذ عليهم الشيطان سُنَن نبي الله وراء ظهورهم ومالوا إلى أسلاف لهم قلدوهم بدينهم، ودانوا بديانتهم، وأبطلوا سُنَنَ رسول الله ، ورفضوها وأنكروها وجحدوها، افتراء منهم على الله، قَدْ ضَلُّوا وَمَا كَانُوا مُهْتَدِينَ ) [الأنعام: ١٤٠]. أوصيكم عباد الله بتقوى الله ، وأحذركم الدنيا ، فإنها حلوة خضرَةٌ، تغرُّ أَهْلَها ، وَتَخْدَعُ سكانها ، قال الله تعالى : ( وَاضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا كَمَاء أَنزَلْنَاهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فَاخْتَلَطَ بِهِ نَبَاتُ الْأَرْضِ فَأَصْبَحَ هَشِيمًا تَذْرُوهُ الرِّيحُ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُقْتَدِرًا ﴾ [الكهف : ٤٥]. مَن كَانَ فِيهَا فِي حَبْرَةٍ أَعْقَبَتْهُ بَعْدَهَا عِبْرَةً، وَمَنْ أَعْطَتْهُ مِن سَرَّائِهَا بَطْنًا أَعْقَبَتْهُ مِن ضَرَّائِهَا ظَهْرًا، غَرَّارَةٌ، غُرُورٌ مَا فِيهَا، فَانِيَةٌ، فَانٍ مَا عَلَيْهَا، كَمَا حَكَمَ عَلَيْهَا رَبُّهَا بِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى، إِذْ يَقُولُ :
In His revelation, He sent him to His creation with the radiant light, the luminous lamp, the manifest proofs, the brilliant evidences and signs, and the overwhelming wonders. He delivered to them the messages of God, and he advised His creation, and he strove in God with true striving, and fought against the people of obstinacy until the word of God, mighty and sublime, was fulfilled, and His command became manifest, and the people submitted to the truth entirely until certainty came to him, neither faltering nor falling short. So may the blessings of God be upon him, a guide to guidance, a clarifier against error and blindness, and upon his pure family, and upon his chosen companions, and upon his pure wives, the mothers of the believers. Through him, God made known to us the laws (shara'i'), the rulings (ahkam), the lawful and the unlawful, and through him He explained to us the legislation of Islam, until the dense darkness was cleared from us, and doubts were removed from us through him, and obscurities were unveiled from us, and clear proofs became apparent to us through him. And he brought us a mighty Book: 'Falsehood cannot come to it from before it or behind it; a revelation from the Wise, the Praiseworthy' (Fussilat 42). In it is gathered the knowledge of the first and the last, and through it He perfected the obligations and the religion. It is the straight path of God and His strong rope. Whoever holds fast to it is saved, and whoever opposes it goes astray and errs, and falls into ignorance. And God urged in His Book adherence to the Sunnah of His Messenger, for He, may He be exalted, said: 'And whatever the Messenger gives you, take it, and whatever he forbids you, refrain from it' (al-Hashr 7). And He, may He be exalted, said: 'Let those who oppose his command beware lest a trial befall them or a painful punishment befall them' (al-Nur 63). And He said: 'If they had referred it to the Messenger and to those in authority among them, those who deduce it from them would know it' (al-Nisa' 83). And He said: 'And whatever you differ over, its judgment belongs to God' (al-Shura 10). He said: 'To the Book of God and the Sunnah of His Prophet.'

And He said: 'Nor does he speak from desire. It is only a revelation revealed' (al-Najm 3-4). And He said: 'Say: It is not for me to change it of my own accord. I only follow what is revealed to me' (Yunus 15). And He said: 'The only saying of the believers when they are called to God and His Messenger to judge between them is that they say: We hear and we obey' (al-Nur 51). So He commanded them to hear His words and obey His command and beware of opposing Him. And He said: 'Obey God and obey the Messenger' (al-Nisa' 59). So He commanded them to obey His Messenger, peace be upon him, just as He commanded them to obey Him, and He called them to adhere to the Sunnah of His Prophet, just as He commanded them to act upon His Book.

5. So many of those whose wretchedness prevailed over them, and whom Satan gained mastery over, cast the Sunnahs of the Prophet of God behind their backs, and inclined to certain predecessors whom they blindly followed in their religion, and they adopted their religion as their own, and they nullified the Sunnahs of the Messenger of God, and rejected them, denied them, and disavowed them, fabricating lies against God. 'They have gone astray, and they were not guided' (al-An'am 140). I advise you, servants of God, to fear God, and I warn you against this world, for it is sweet and green, it deceives its people and deludes its inhabitants. God Almighty said: 'And strike for them the example of the life of this world: like water which We send down from the sky, and the vegetation of the earth mixes with it, and then it becomes dry chaff that the winds scatter. And God is over all things competent' (al-Kahf 45). Whoever was in it in joy, it brought him after it a lesson; and whoever it gave from its pleasures a bellyful, it brought upon him from its hardships a backful. It is a deceiver; what is in it is delusion; it is perishable; whatever is on it is passing away. As its Lord has decreed concerning it with His saying, when He says: ...
Source pages p. 35

Commentary
This passage is an exhortation by Imam al-Ash'ari on the obligation to follow the Quran and Sunnah and a warning against those who abandon them. The passage begins by praising the Prophet Muhammad's mission: he was sent with clear proofs and overwhelming signs, fulfilled God's command, and guided the people to truth. Al-Ash'ari then highlights the role of the Prophet in explaining the laws and rulings of Islam, dispelling ignorance and doubts. The Quran is described as a mighty book that contains knowledge of all things and is the straight path and strong rope of God; clinging to it ensures salvation, while opposing it leads to error. Al-Ash'ari then cites multiple Quranic verses commanding obedience to the Prophet and adherence to his Sunnah, emphasizing that the Prophet's words are revelation and must be followed. The second part of the passage (section 5) condemns those who abandon the Sunnah and blindly follow their ancestors, accusing them of fabricating lies against God and going astray. Finally, al-Ash'ari delivers a warning about the deceptive nature of this world, citing the Quranic parable of life being like vegetation that withers away, and reminds the audience that worldly joys are temporary and will be followed by hardship. The passage ends abruptly at a verse citation, but the overall message is clear: hold fast to the Quran and Sunnah, beware of blind imitation, and do not be deceived by this world.

Key Terms
تقليد
سنة

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Blindly following scholars (taqlid) is always acceptable, even if it contradicts the Sunnah.
Correction: Al-Ash'ari condemns those who abandon the Sunnah for blind imitation of predecessors, calling it misguidance and fabrication against God.

Checkpoint
According to the passage, what do those who abandon the Sunnah and blindly follow their predecessors do?
A) They become more knowledgeable.
B) They nullify and reject the Sunnahs of the Prophet.
C) They innovate beneficial practices.
D) They follow a middle path.

Key Takeaway
Abandoning the Sunnah for blind imitation of ancestors is a grave error that leads to misguidance and fabrication against God.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
Is there any ancestral tradition or cultural practice in your life that you hold onto even when you know it contradicts a clear Sunnah? Will you have the courage to abandon it today?
?
When you hear a hadith or learn a Sunnah, do you ever hesitate because it conflicts with what your family or community has taught you? What will you do the next time that happens?
?
The passage says those who abandon the Sunnah are overcome by wretchedness and Satan. Have you ever felt that your attachment to your inherited ways has dulled your sensitivity to the Quran and Sunnah? How will you actively re-prioritize the Sunnah starting now?

Prophethood
beginner

Warning Against the Deception of This World
Al-Ash'ari warns about the deceptive nature of this world and its fleeting pleasures.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand al-Ash'ari's warning about the fleeting and deceptive nature of this world and the importance of prioritizing the Hereafter.

Core Idea
This world is sweet and green but deceives; its pleasures are temporary and lead to hardship.

Imam al-Ash'ari concludes his exhortation with a poignant warning about the world. He describes it as sweet and green, yet deceptive, and he quotes the Quranic parable of life like vegetation that withers. This lesson serves as a reminder to remain vigilant and not be deluded by worldly attractions.

Guided Path
1
Read the warning passage
Read the Arabic passage beginning with 'I advise you, servants of God...' and its translation.
Note the description of the world: 'sweet and green, deceives its people.'
2
Understand the Quranic parable
Al-Ash'ari quotes the verse from Surah al-Kahf comparing the world to water that brings vegetation then becomes chaff. Reflect on the impermanence.
3
Internalize the lesson
The passage ends with describing the world as a deceiver and perishable. This should motivate one to focus on eternal matters.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
على وحيه، أرسله إلى خلقه بالنور الساطع، والسراج اللامع، والحجج الظاهرة، والبراهين والآيات الباهرة، والأعاجيب القاهرة، فبلغ عن الله رسالاته، ونصح له في بريَّاته، وجاهد في الله حق الجهاد، وقاتل أهل العناد، حتى تمت كلمة الله عَزَّ وجل، وظَهَرَ أَمْرُه، وانقاد الناس للحق أجمعين حتى أتاه اليقين لا وانياً ولا مقصراً فصلوات الله عليه من قائد إلى الهدى، مبين عن ضلالة وعمي وعلى آل بيته الطيبين، وعلى أصحابه المنتخبين، وعلى أزواجه الطاهرات أمهات المؤمنين . عرفنا الله به الشرائع والأحكام والحلال والحرام، وبين لنا به شريعة الإسلام ، حتى انجلت عنا طخياء الظلام، وانحسرت به عنا الشبهات، وانكشفت به عنا الغيابات وظهرت لنا به البينات . وجاءنا بكتاب عزيز ( لَا يَأْتِيهِ الْبَطِلُ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَلَا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ تَنزِيلٌ مِنْ حَكِيمٍ حميد [فصلت: ٤٢] جمع فيه علم الأولين والآخرين، وأكمل به الفرائض والدين، فهو صراط الله المستقيم، وحبله المتين، من تمسك به نجا، ومن خالفه ضل وغوى، وفي الجهل تَرَدَّى ، وحث الله في كتابه على التمسك بسنة رسوله ، فقال عز وجل: ﴿وَمَا آتَاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَمَا نَهَاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانتَهُوا ) [الحشر: ۷] وقال عز وجل: فَلْيَحْذَرِ الَّذِينَ يُخَالِفُونَ عَنْ أَمْرِهِ أَن تُصِيبَهُمْ فِتْنَةٌ أَوْ يُصِيبَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ [النور: ٦٣]. وقال: ﴿وَلَوْ رَدُّوهُ إِلَى الرَّسُولِ وَإِلَى أُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْهُمْ لَعَلِمَهُ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَنْبِطُونَهُ مِنْهُمْ ﴾ [النساء:۸۳] . وقال : ( وَمَا اخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِيهِ مِن شَيْءٍ فَحُكْمُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ ﴾ [الشورى: ١٠] ، يقول : إلى كتاب الله وسنة نبيه

وقال: ﴿ وَمَا يَنطِقُ عَنِ الْهَوَى * إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا وَحْيٌ يُوحَى ) [ النجم : ٣-٤] ، وقال : ( قُل مَا يَكُونُ لِي أَنْ أُبَدِّلَهُ مِن تِلْقَاءِ نَفْسِي إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا مَا يُوحَى إِلَيَّ ) [يونس : ١٥] . وقال : إِنَّمَا كَانَ قَوْلَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذَا دُعُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَن يَقُولُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا ) [ النور : ٥١]، فأمرهم أن يسمعوا قوله، ويطيعوا أمره، ويحذروا مخالفته. وقال : أَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ ﴾ [النساء : ٥٩] ، فأمرهم بطاعةِ رسولِهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم كما أمرهم بطاعته، ودعاهم إلى التمسك بسنة نبيه ، كما أمرهم بالعمل بكتابه.

٥ - فَنَبَذَ كثير ممن غلبت عليهم شقوتهم ، واستحوذ عليهم الشيطان سُنَن نبي الله وراء ظهورهم ومالوا إلى أسلاف لهم قلدوهم بدينهم، ودانوا بديانتهم، وأبطلوا سُنَنَ رسول الله ، ورفضوها وأنكروها وجحدوها، افتراء منهم على الله، قَدْ ضَلُّوا وَمَا كَانُوا مُهْتَدِينَ ) [الأنعام: ١٤٠]. أوصيكم عباد الله بتقوى الله ، وأحذركم الدنيا ، فإنها حلوة خضرَةٌ، تغرُّ أَهْلَها ، وَتَخْدَعُ سكانها ، قال الله تعالى : ( وَاضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا كَمَاء أَنزَلْنَاهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فَاخْتَلَطَ بِهِ نَبَاتُ الْأَرْضِ فَأَصْبَحَ هَشِيمًا تَذْرُوهُ الرِّيحُ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُقْتَدِرًا ﴾ [الكهف : ٤٥]. مَن كَانَ فِيهَا فِي حَبْرَةٍ أَعْقَبَتْهُ بَعْدَهَا عِبْرَةً، وَمَنْ أَعْطَتْهُ مِن سَرَّائِهَا بَطْنًا أَعْقَبَتْهُ مِن ضَرَّائِهَا ظَهْرًا، غَرَّارَةٌ، غُرُورٌ مَا فِيهَا، فَانِيَةٌ، فَانٍ مَا عَلَيْهَا، كَمَا حَكَمَ عَلَيْهَا رَبُّهَا بِقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى، إِذْ يَقُولُ :
In His revelation, He sent him to His creation with the radiant light, the luminous lamp, the manifest proofs, the brilliant evidences and signs, and the overwhelming wonders. He delivered to them the messages of God, and he advised His creation, and he strove in God with true striving, and fought against the people of obstinacy until the word of God, mighty and sublime, was fulfilled, and His command became manifest, and the people submitted to the truth entirely until certainty came to him, neither faltering nor falling short. So may the blessings of God be upon him, a guide to guidance, a clarifier against error and blindness, and upon his pure family, and upon his chosen companions, and upon his pure wives, the mothers of the believers. Through him, God made known to us the laws (shara'i'), the rulings (ahkam), the lawful and the unlawful, and through him He explained to us the legislation of Islam, until the dense darkness was cleared from us, and doubts were removed from us through him, and obscurities were unveiled from us, and clear proofs became apparent to us through him. And he brought us a mighty Book: 'Falsehood cannot come to it from before it or behind it; a revelation from the Wise, the Praiseworthy' (Fussilat 42). In it is gathered the knowledge of the first and the last, and through it He perfected the obligations and the religion. It is the straight path of God and His strong rope. Whoever holds fast to it is saved, and whoever opposes it goes astray and errs, and falls into ignorance. And God urged in His Book adherence to the Sunnah of His Messenger, for He, may He be exalted, said: 'And whatever the Messenger gives you, take it, and whatever he forbids you, refrain from it' (al-Hashr 7). And He, may He be exalted, said: 'Let those who oppose his command beware lest a trial befall them or a painful punishment befall them' (al-Nur 63). And He said: 'If they had referred it to the Messenger and to those in authority among them, those who deduce it from them would know it' (al-Nisa' 83). And He said: 'And whatever you differ over, its judgment belongs to God' (al-Shura 10). He said: 'To the Book of God and the Sunnah of His Prophet.'

And He said: 'Nor does he speak from desire. It is only a revelation revealed' (al-Najm 3-4). And He said: 'Say: It is not for me to change it of my own accord. I only follow what is revealed to me' (Yunus 15). And He said: 'The only saying of the believers when they are called to God and His Messenger to judge between them is that they say: We hear and we obey' (al-Nur 51). So He commanded them to hear His words and obey His command and beware of opposing Him. And He said: 'Obey God and obey the Messenger' (al-Nisa' 59). So He commanded them to obey His Messenger, peace be upon him, just as He commanded them to obey Him, and He called them to adhere to the Sunnah of His Prophet, just as He commanded them to act upon His Book.

5. So many of those whose wretchedness prevailed over them, and whom Satan gained mastery over, cast the Sunnahs of the Prophet of God behind their backs, and inclined to certain predecessors whom they blindly followed in their religion, and they adopted their religion as their own, and they nullified the Sunnahs of the Messenger of God, and rejected them, denied them, and disavowed them, fabricating lies against God. 'They have gone astray, and they were not guided' (al-An'am 140). I advise you, servants of God, to fear God, and I warn you against this world, for it is sweet and green, it deceives its people and deludes its inhabitants. God Almighty said: 'And strike for them the example of the life of this world: like water which We send down from the sky, and the vegetation of the earth mixes with it, and then it becomes dry chaff that the winds scatter. And God is over all things competent' (al-Kahf 45). Whoever was in it in joy, it brought him after it a lesson; and whoever it gave from its pleasures a bellyful, it brought upon him from its hardships a backful. It is a deceiver; what is in it is delusion; it is perishable; whatever is on it is passing away. As its Lord has decreed concerning it with His saying, when He says: ...
Source pages p. 35

Commentary
This passage is an exhortation by Imam al-Ash'ari on the obligation to follow the Quran and Sunnah and a warning against those who abandon them. The passage begins by praising the Prophet Muhammad's mission: he was sent with clear proofs and overwhelming signs, fulfilled God's command, and guided the people to truth. Al-Ash'ari then highlights the role of the Prophet in explaining the laws and rulings of Islam, dispelling ignorance and doubts. The Quran is described as a mighty book that contains knowledge of all things and is the straight path and strong rope of God; clinging to it ensures salvation, while opposing it leads to error. Al-Ash'ari then cites multiple Quranic verses commanding obedience to the Prophet and adherence to his Sunnah, emphasizing that the Prophet's words are revelation and must be followed. The second part of the passage (section 5) condemns those who abandon the Sunnah and blindly follow their ancestors, accusing them of fabricating lies against God and going astray. Finally, al-Ash'ari delivers a warning about the deceptive nature of this world, citing the Quranic parable of life being like vegetation that withers away, and reminds the audience that worldly joys are temporary and will be followed by hardship. The passage ends abruptly at a verse citation, but the overall message is clear: hold fast to the Quran and Sunnah, beware of blind imitation, and do not be deceived by this world.

Key Terms
دنيا

Key Terms from Source Passage

Checkpoint
What analogy does the Quran use for the life of this world, as quoted in the passage?
A) A beautiful garden that lasts forever.
B) Water that brings vegetation then becomes dry chaff.
C) A race with a prize at the end.
D) A shadow that never disappears.

Key Takeaway
This world is temporary and deceptive; believers should not be deluded by its fleeting pleasures but focus on the eternal Hereafter.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
Think of one area of your life where you are currently chasing something 'sweet and green'—like status, wealth, or a relationship—that you know in your heart is temporary. What specific thing is it? Are you willing to take a concrete step today to loosen its grip on your heart? What will that step be?
?
The lesson says: 'Whoever was in it in joy, it brought him after it a lesson.' Look back at a past joy or achievement that later turned into hardship or regret. Did that experience truly teach you to distrust the world, or have you fallen back into the same pursuit? If you haven't changed, what is stopping you from learning that lesson now?
?
Al-Ash'ari advises you to fear God and warns you against this world. Imagine you are standing before Allah tomorrow being asked about your attachments to the world. What would you point to as the thing you loved most that distracted you from the Hereafter? Now, what is one action you can take this week to prioritize your relationship with Allah over that thing?

The Denial of the Prophet's Intercession
Al-Ash'ari accuses the Mu'tazila of denying intercession and rejecting narrations.

Learning Objective
By the end of this lesson, you will understand that the deviant sects deny the Prophet's intercession for sinners and reject the transmitted evidence from the early Muslims.

Core Idea
Denying intercession means rejecting authentic transmitted knowledge.

Al-Ash'ari begins his critique of the Mu'tazila by highlighting a crucial belief: the intercession of the Prophet for sinners. He doesn't just state their denial; he points out that they also actively reject the narrations from the early generations that confirm this blessing. This shows that their error is not just in doctrine but in methodology — they abandon transmitted texts for rational opinion.

Guided Path
1
Read the source passage
Read the Arabic sentence and its translation carefully. Notice that al-Ash'ari mentions two actions: denial of intercession and rejection of narrations.
2
Understand the two-fold accusation
Al-Ash'ari accuses the deviant sects of both denying the intercession and rejecting the narrations that support it. Consider why he mentions both — the second accusation highlights their rejection of transmitted evidence.
3
Reflect on the implications
Ask yourself: What does it mean to reject narrations from the salaf? How does that affect a person's faith? This connects to the larger theme of relying on revelation versus reason alone.

Source Text
⚠ Extracted via Advanced OCR ∼97% accuracy, minor errors may remain — always verify against original Arabic books
وأنكروا شفاعة رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم للمذنبين، وردوا الروايات في ذلك عن السلف المتقدمين.
And they denied the intercession of the Messenger of Allah, may Allah bless him and grant him peace, for the sinners, and they rejected the narrations concerning that from the early predecessors.
Source pages p. 38

Commentary
This is a brief but clear accusation. Al-Ash'ari states that the Mu'tazila and Qadariyya reject the intercession (shafa'a) of the Prophet for sinners. Moreover, they actively reject the transmitted reports (riwayat) from the early generations that confirm such intercession. This highlights their reliance on opinion over transmitted knowledge.

Key Terms
الشفاعة
المذنبين
الروايات
السلف

Key Terms from Source Passage

Misconception & Correction
Misconception: Some might think intercession is denied on purely rational grounds, as if the Mu'tazila had good reasons.
Correction: Al-Ash'ari points out that they also reject the transmitted evidence, indicating that their error is in refusing to accept authentic narrations, not just in reasoning.

Checkpoint
What do the deviant sects do with the narrations about intercession according to al-Ash'ari?
A) Accept them fully
B) Reject them
C) Interpret them metaphorically
D) Add to them

Key Takeaway
Denial of intercession is coupled with rejection of transmitted evidence, showing a methodological deviation.

How This Lesson Applies — to MY Life?
?
When you encounter a hadith or a teaching from the salaf that challenges your intuition, do you find yourself subtly dismissing it or seeking ways to reinterpret it? How does this passage expose the danger of prioritizing your own reasoning over transmitted knowledge? Take a moment to identify one such teaching you have struggled with and commit to re-examining it with an open heart.
?
Are there any areas of your faith where you have unknowingly relied more on cultural or intellectual trends than on the authentic narrations from the early Muslims? This passage warns that rejecting narrations can lead to denying core beliefs. What step will you take today to strengthen your trust in the transmitted tradition?
